title.zh: 卷二十四下 食貨志
title.raw: 卷二十四下 食貨志 Volume 24b: Treatise on Trade 2

sentences.zh: 凡貨，金錢布帛之用，夏殷以前其詳靡記云。

translations.literal: As for currency-the use of gold, coin, cloth, and silk-before Xia and Shang its details are not well recorded.
translations.idiomatic: The monetary system before Xia and Shang is poorly documented, though gold, coin, and textiles were already in use.

sentences.zh: 太公為周立九府圜法：黃金方寸，而重一斤；

translations.literal: Taigong established for Zhou the Nine Offices round-law: one square inch of yellow gold weighed one jin;
translations.idiomatic: Jiang Taigong is said to have set Zhou's basic monetary standards, beginning with a fixed gold weight.

sentences.zh: 錢圜函方，輕重以銖；

translations.literal: coins were round outside and square within, their lightness and heaviness measured in zhu;
translations.idiomatic: Coin form and weight were standardized down to fine zhu units.

sentences.zh: 布帛廣二尺二寸為幅，長四丈為匹。

translations.literal: cloth two chi two cun wide made one bolt, and four zhang long made one piece.
translations.idiomatic: Textiles were likewise standardized by width and length for value exchange.

sentences.zh: 故貨寶於金，利於刀，流於泉，布於布，束於帛。

translations.literal: Thus wealth was treasured in gold, advantage in knife-money, circulation in spring-coin, spread in cloth, bundled in silk.
translations.idiomatic: Different media carried different monetary functions: storage, transaction, circulation, and bundling.

sentences.zh: 太公退，又行之于齊。

translations.literal: After Taigong retired, he again practiced this in Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Taigong later implemented similar principles in the state of Qi.

sentences.zh: 至管仲相桓公，通輕重之權，曰：「歲有凶穰，故穀有貴賤；

translations.literal: By the time Guan Zhong assisted Duke Huan, he mastered the power of light and heavy, saying: 'Years have scarcity and abundance, so grain has high and low prices;
translations.idiomatic: Under Guan Zhong, Qi developed active price and supply management based on harvest cycles.

sentences.zh: 令有緩急，故物有輕重。

translations.literal: orders have urgency and slack, so things have lightness and heaviness.
translations.idiomatic: Policy pressure itself also shifts relative prices.

sentences.zh: 人君不理，則畜賈游於市，乘民之不給，百倍其本矣。

translations.literal: If the ruler does not manage this, hoarding merchants roam markets, exploiting shortages and multiplying principal a hundredfold.
translations.idiomatic: Without state intervention, speculative merchants exploit scarcity for extreme profits.

sentences.zh: 故萬乘之國必有萬金之賈，千乘之國必有千金之賈者，利有所并也。

translations.literal: Therefore a ten-thousand-chariot state must have ten-thousand-gold merchants, a thousand-chariot state must have thousand-gold merchants-because gains are concentrated.
translations.idiomatic: Large polities inevitably generate large-scale merchants when rents are left unchecked.

sentences.zh: 計本量委則足矣。

translations.literal: If one calculates base and measures accumulation, it is enough.
translations.idiomatic: Proper accounting of stock and flow is sufficient for stabilization.

sentences.zh: 然而民有飢餓者，穀有所臧也。

translations.literal: Yet people may still be hungry because grain is hoarded.
translations.idiomatic: Famine can persist even amid aggregate supply when hoarding blocks access.

sentences.zh: 民有餘則輕之，故人君斂之以輕；

translations.literal: When people have surplus, prices are light, so rulers collect with light prices;
translations.idiomatic: In surplus years the state should buy at low prices;

sentences.zh: 民不足則重之，故人君散之以重。

translations.literal: when people lack, prices are heavy, so rulers release with heavy prices.
translations.idiomatic: in shortage years it should release stock at high market moments.

sentences.zh: 凡輕重斂散之以時，則準平。

translations.literal: If collecting and releasing by light and heavy are timed, standards become level.
translations.idiomatic: Timed countercyclical buying and selling stabilizes prices.

sentences.zh: 使萬室之邑必有萬鍾之臧，臧繈千萬；

translations.literal: Let a ten-thousand-household city have ten-thousand-zhong stores and ten-million cash strings in reserve;
translations.idiomatic: Large urban centers should hold commensurate grain and cash reserves;

sentences.zh: 千室之邑必有千鍾之臧，臧繈百萬。

translations.literal: a thousand-household city have thousand-zhong stores and one-million cash strings.
translations.idiomatic: smaller cities should hold proportionate reserves.

sentences.zh: 春以奉耕，夏以奉耘，耒耜器械，種饟糧食，必取澹焉。

translations.literal: In spring provide for plowing, in summer for weeding-plows, tools, seed and food supplies-must all be drawn from these stores.
translations.idiomatic: These reserves finance seasonal agricultural inputs and subsistence.

sentences.zh: 故大賈畜家不得豪奪吾民矣。」

translations.literal: Then great merchant houses cannot violently seize our people.
translations.idiomatic: With state-backed provisioning, large merchants cannot coerce the peasantry.

sentences.zh: 桓公遂用區區之齊合諸侯，顯伯名。

translations.literal: Duke Huan then used small Qi to unite the lords and make his hegemonic name manifest.
translations.idiomatic: Qi's hegemony was built on this economic statecraft.

sentences.zh: 其後百餘年，周景王時患錢輕，將更鑄大錢，單穆公曰：「不可。

translations.literal: Over a century later, in King Jing of Zhou's time, he worried coins were too light and planned to cast larger coins; Duke Shan Mu said: 'Impossible.'
translations.idiomatic: When Zhou sought to debase this balance by recasting heavy coin, officials warned against it.

sentences.zh: 古者天降災戾，於是乎量資幣，權輕重，以救民。

translations.literal: 'In antiquity when Heaven sent calamity, one then measured currency and weighted light and heavy to rescue the people.
translations.idiomatic: Historically, monetary adjustment was a relief tool during crisis.

sentences.zh: 民患輕，則為之作重幣以行之，於是有母權子而行，民皆得焉。

translations.literal: When people suffered from light coin, heavy coin was made and circulated, so mother weighted child and both were usable.
translations.idiomatic: He argued for coexistence of denominations: heavy units could discipline light units.

sentences.zh: 若不堪重，則多作輕而行之，亦不廢重，於是乎有子權母而行，小大利之。

translations.literal: If heavy coin was hard to bear, then more light coin circulated, yet heavy was not abolished; thus child weighted mother and both benefited, especially small profits.
translations.idiomatic: Conversely, light units could expand liquidity without eliminating heavy anchors.

sentences.zh: 今王廢輕而作重，民失其資，能無匱乎？

translations.literal: Now if the king abolishes light and makes only heavy, people lose means-how can there be no shortage?
translations.idiomatic: Eliminating light denominations would create transactional scarcity.

sentences.zh: 民若匱，王用將有所乏；

translations.literal: If people are short, the king's use will also be lacking;
translations.idiomatic: Public shortage feeds directly into fiscal shortage;

sentences.zh: 乏將厚取於民；

translations.literal: lacking, he will take more heavily from the people;
translations.idiomatic: the state then compensates by over-extraction;

sentences.zh: 民不給，將有遠志，是離民也。

translations.literal: people cannot supply and will have outward intentions-this separates people from ruler.
translations.idiomatic: which drives disaffection and exit behavior.

sentences.zh: 且絕民以實王府，猶塞川原為潢洿也，竭亡日矣。

translations.literal: And to cut off people to fill royal stores is like damming river source into ponds-exhaustion is near.
translations.idiomatic: Draining society to fill the treasury is fiscally self-defeating.

sentences.zh: 王其圖之。」

translations.literal: The king should consider it.'
translations.idiomatic: He urged strategic restraint.

sentences.zh: 弗聽，卒鑄大錢，文曰「寶貨」，肉好皆有周郭，以勸農澹不足，百姓蒙利焉。

translations.literal: He did not listen, finally cast large coin inscribed 'Treasure Currency,' with broad rim and hole; it was said to encourage agriculture and relieve scarcity, and people benefited.
translations.idiomatic: Despite objections, larger coinage was issued and temporarily framed as pro-agrarian stabilization.

sentences.zh: 秦兼天下，幣為二等：黃金以溢為名，上幣；

translations.literal: When Qin unified the world, currency had two grades: yellow gold by yi was upper currency;
translations.idiomatic: Qin standardized a two-tier monetary system headed by gold.

sentences.zh: 銅錢質如周錢，文曰「半兩」，重如其文。

translations.literal: copper coins were like Zhou coin, inscribed 'half-liang,' weighing as inscribed.
translations.idiomatic: Base coin was the half-liang copper unit with nominal full-weight specification.

sentences.zh: 而珠玉龜貝銀錫之屬為器飾寶臧，不為幣，然各隨時而輕重無常。

translations.literal: Pearls, jade, turtle shell, shells, silver, tin served as ornaments and stores, not currency, and their values varied with time.
translations.idiomatic: Luxury stores of value remained outside official currency and fluctuated freely.

sentences.zh: 漢興，以為秦錢重難用，更令民鑄莢錢。

translations.literal: When Han rose, it judged Qin coin too heavy and hard to use, so ordered people to cast pod-coins.
translations.idiomatic: Early Han lightened circulation by introducing smaller pod-like coins.

sentences.zh: 黃金一斤。

translations.literal: Gold remained one jin.
translations.idiomatic: The gold standard unit stayed at one jin.

sentences.zh: 而不軌逐利之民畜積餘贏以稽市物，痛騰躍，米至石萬錢，馬至匹百金。

translations.literal: But unruly profit-seeking people hoarded surplus to corner market goods; prices violently jumped, rice reaching ten-thousand cash per shi and horses one hundred gold per head.
translations.idiomatic: Speculative hoarding triggered extreme inflation in grain and horse markets.

sentences.zh: 天下已平，高祖乃令賈人不得衣絲乘車，重稅租以困辱之。

translations.literal: After the realm was pacified, Gaozu ordered merchants not to wear silk or ride carriages, and imposed heavy taxes to constrain and disgrace them.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu used sumptuary and tax policy to politically subordinate merchants.

sentences.zh: 孝惠、高后時，為天下初定，復弛商賈之律，然市井子孫亦不得宦為吏。

translations.literal: In Hui and Empress Gao's era, as the realm was newly settled, merchant restrictions were relaxed, yet market households' descendants still could not become officials.
translations.idiomatic: Later easing preserved one key barrier: merchant families remained largely excluded from office.

sentences.zh: 孝文五年，為錢益多而輕，乃更鑄四銖錢，其文為「半兩」。

translations.literal: In Wen year five, because money grew too abundant and light, four-zhu coins were recast inscribed 'half-liang.'
translations.idiomatic: As coin quality degraded, the court reissued heavier-denomination copper.

sentences.zh: 除盜鑄錢令，使民放鑄。

translations.literal: The law against illicit coin casting was removed, allowing people to cast freely.
translations.idiomatic: Private minting was legalized in an attempt to ease circulation.

sentences.zh: 賈誼諫曰：

translations.literal: Jia Yi remonstrated:
translations.idiomatic: Jia Yi objected strongly.

sentences.zh: 法使天下公得顧租鑄銅錫為錢，敢雜以鉛鐵為它巧者，其罪黥。

translations.literal: The law lets all under heaven publicly hire and rent to cast copper and tin as coin; those daring to mix lead or iron by trick are tattoo-punished.
translations.idiomatic: The law nominally criminalized adulteration, with tattoo punishment for mixers of base metals.

sentences.zh: 然鑄錢之情，非殽雜為巧，則不可得贏；

translations.literal: But in the reality of coin casting, without adulterating for trick one cannot profit;
translations.idiomatic: Yet profitability in private minting almost required debasement.

sentences.zh: 而殽之甚微，為利甚厚。

translations.literal: and when adulteration is tiny, profit is huge.
translations.idiomatic: Even slight debasement yielded large margins.

sentences.zh: 夫事有召禍而法有起姦，今令細民人操造幣之勢，各隱屏而鑄作，因欲禁其厚利微姦，雖黥罪日報，其勢不止。

translations.literal: When policy invites disaster and law generates crime: now allowing petty people control of coin production, each casting in hiding, wanting to ban tiny fraud and huge profit-even if tattoo penalties are reported daily, the trend will not stop.
translations.idiomatic: Jia Yi argued the policy itself created crime incentives that punishment could not suppress.

sentences.zh: 乃者，民人抵罪，多者一縣百數，及吏之所疑，榜笞奔走者甚眾。

translations.literal: Recently convicted people are many, in some counties over a hundred, and those officials suspect and flog while pursuing are numerous.
translations.idiomatic: Enforcement costs exploded into mass arrest and coercive investigation.

sentences.zh: 夫縣法以誘民，使入陷阱，孰積於此！

translations.literal: To use county law to lure people into traps-what accumulation could exceed this!
translations.idiomatic: He condemned the system as structurally entrapping the populace.

sentences.zh: 曩禁鑄錢，死罪積下；

translations.literal: Formerly banning coin casting accumulated death penalties;
translations.idiomatic: Under prohibition, capital cases piled up;

sentences.zh: 今公鑄錢，黥罪積下。

translations.literal: now public casting accumulates tattoo penalties.
translations.idiomatic: under legalization, tattoo penalties piled up instead.

sentences.zh: 為法若此，上何賴焉？

translations.literal: With law like this, what can the ruler rely on?
translations.idiomatic: Either way, legitimacy and governability were undermined.

sentences.zh: 又民用錢，郡縣不同：或用錢輕，百加若干；

translations.literal: Also people use coin differently by commandery and county: some use light coin and add premium per hundred;
translations.idiomatic: Regional coin standards diverged with ad hoc exchange premiums;

sentences.zh: 或用重錢，平稱不受。

translations.literal: some use heavy coin and refuse equal-weight exchange.
translations.idiomatic: others rejected parity conversion entirely.

sentences.zh: 法錢不立，吏急而壹之虖，則大為煩苛，而力不能勝；

translations.literal: When legal coin standards are unsettled, if officials rush to unify it, burden and harshness greatly increase and capability cannot sustain;
translations.idiomatic: Rigid forced standardization risked administrative overload and coercion.

sentences.zh: 縱而弗呵虖，則市肆異用，錢文大亂。

translations.literal: if relaxed without prohibition, markets use mixed systems and coin inscriptions become chaotic.
translations.idiomatic: But laissez-faire produced monetary fragmentation.

sentences.zh: 苟非其術，何鄉而可哉！

translations.literal: If not this method, where can it work?
translations.idiomatic: He concluded the current policy path was untenable.

sentences.zh: 今農事棄捐而采銅者日蕃，釋其耒耨，冶鎔炊炭，姦錢日多，五穀不為多。

translations.literal: Now farming is abandoned while copper mining grows daily; plows and hoes are laid aside, smelting and charcoal rise, bad coin increases daily, and the five grains do not increase.
translations.idiomatic: Coin incentives were diverting labor from agriculture into mining and minting, without raising food output.

sentences.zh: 善人怵而為姦邪，愿民陷而之刑戮，刑戮將甚不詳，奈何而忽！

translations.literal: Good people become fearful and turn to wickedness; simple people fall into punishment and execution; penalties will become gravely unregulated-how can this be ignored!
translations.idiomatic: He warned of moral inversion and escalating penal arbitrariness.

sentences.zh: 國知患此，吏議必曰禁之。

translations.literal: The state knows this harm; officials will surely say 'ban it.'
translations.idiomatic: The predictable bureaucratic response was outright prohibition.

sentences.zh: 禁之不得其術，其傷必大。

translations.literal: But if banning does not get the method right, the injury must be great.
translations.idiomatic: A poorly designed ban would cause even larger damage.

sentences.zh: 令禁鑄錢，則錢必重；

translations.literal: If coin casting is banned, coin must become heavy;
translations.idiomatic: Ban-induced scarcity would raise coin value sharply;

sentences.zh: 重則其利深，盜鑄如雲而起，棄市之罪又不足以禁矣。

translations.literal: when heavy, profits deepen, illicit casting rises like clouds, and even market execution cannot ban it.
translations.idiomatic: which would intensify black-market minting beyond deterrence.

sentences.zh: 姦數不勝而法禁數潰，銅使之然也。

translations.literal: Crimes too many to count while legal bans repeatedly collapse-this is caused by copper itself.
translations.idiomatic: The commodity nature of copper made repeated prohibition cycles unstable.

sentences.zh: 故銅布於天下，其為禍博矣。

translations.literal: Thus copper spread through the realm has broad potential for harm.
translations.idiomatic: In his view, unrestricted copper monetization carried systemic risk.

sentences.zh: 今博禍可除，而七福可致也。

translations.literal: Now broad harm can be removed and seven benefits achieved.
translations.idiomatic: He proposed an alternative promising seven concrete gains.

sentences.zh: 何謂七福？

translations.literal: What are the seven benefits?
translations.idiomatic: He then enumerated them.

sentences.zh: 上收銅勿令布，則民不鑄錢，黥罪不積，一矣。

translations.literal: If the state collects copper and does not let it circulate, people will not cast coin, and tattoo penalties will not accumulate-first.
translations.idiomatic: First, centralized copper control would suppress private mint crime.

sentences.zh: 偽錢不蕃，民不相疑，二矣。

translations.literal: Counterfeit coin will not proliferate, and people will not suspect one another-second.
translations.idiomatic: Second, trust in exchange would recover as fake coin declines.

sentences.zh: 采銅鑄作者反於耕田，三矣。

translations.literal: Copper miners and casters return to field cultivation-third.
translations.idiomatic: Third, labor would return from minting to agriculture.

sentences.zh: 銅畢歸於上，上挾銅積以御輕重，錢輕則以術斂之，重則以術散之，貨物必平，四矣。

translations.literal: Copper all returning above, the ruler uses copper reserves to control light and heavy: if coin light use method to collect, if heavy use method to release, goods must level-fourth.
translations.idiomatic: Fourth, with metal monopoly the state can run true countercyclical monetary operations to stabilize prices.

sentences.zh: 以作兵器，以假貴臣，多少有制，用別貴踐，五矣。

translations.literal: Using it to make weapons and lend to nobles in regulated amounts, with use distinguishing rank and status-fifth.
translations.idiomatic: Fifth, copper can be allocated strategically for arms and rank-differentiated state supply.

sentences.zh: 以臨萬貨，以調盈虛，以收奇羨，則官富實而末民困，六矣。

translations.literal: Using it to supervise all goods, adjust surplus and shortage, and collect extraordinary excess, then state offices become rich and nonproductive people constrained-sixth.
translations.idiomatic: Sixth, the state gains fiscal steering capacity while curbing speculative parasitism.

sentences.zh: 制吾棄財，以與匈奴逐爭其民，其敵必懷，七矣。

translations.literal: Controlling our expendable wealth to contend with Xiongnu and draw away their people, the enemy will surely be affected-seventh.
translations.idiomatic: Seventh, monetary leverage could be used geopolitically against the Xiongnu frontier system.

sentences.zh: 故善為天下者，因禍而為福，轉敗而為功。

translations.literal: Thus those good at ruling the world turn disaster into benefit and defeat into achievement.
translations.idiomatic: Good governance converts structural liabilities into strategic advantage.

sentences.zh: 今久退七福而行博禍，臣誠傷之。

translations.literal: Now for long we have retreated from seven benefits and practiced broad harm-I truly grieve this.
translations.idiomatic: He closes by lamenting persistent refusal of reform.

sentences.zh: 上不聽。

translations.literal: The emperor did not listen.
translations.idiomatic: The court rejected Jia Yi's proposal.

sentences.zh: 是時，吳以諸侯即山鑄錢，富埒天子，後卒叛逆。

translations.literal: At that time, Wu as feudal state cast coins from mountains, wealth matching the Son of Heaven, and later finally rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: Wu's independent minting power enriched it to near-imperial scale and fed later rebellion.

sentences.zh: 鄧通，大夫也，以鑄錢財過王者。

translations.literal: Deng Tong, though a grandee, in coin-casting wealth exceeded kings.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Tong's private mint fortune became politically notorious.

sentences.zh: 故吳、鄧錢布天下。

translations.literal: Thus Wu and Deng coin circulated under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Their privately associated currencies spread widely.

sentences.zh: 其後，衛青歲以數萬騎出擊匈奴，遂取河南地，築朔方。

translations.literal: Afterward, each year Wei Qing led tens of thousands of cavalry to strike Xiongnu, taking Henan lands and building Shuofang.
translations.idiomatic: Major anti-Xiongnu campaigns under Wei Qing expanded territory and frontier fortification.

sentences.zh: 時又通西南夷道，作者數萬人，千里負擔餽饟，率十餘鍾致一石，散幣於邛僰以輯之。

translations.literal: At that time roads to southwestern tribes were opened, with tens of thousands of laborers, carrying burdens for a thousand li where over ten zhong sent only one shi delivered, and currency was dispersed among Qiong and Bo to placate them.
translations.idiomatic: Southwestern expansion was logistically exorbitant, with huge transport losses and costly pacification payments.

sentences.zh: 數歲而道不通，蠻夷因以數攻吏，吏發兵誅之。

translations.literal: After several years roads still not open, and tribes repeatedly attacked officials; officials sent troops to punish them.
translations.idiomatic: The project failed to stabilize routes and triggered recurring military retaliation.

sentences.zh: 悉巴蜀租賦不足以更之，乃募豪民田南夷，入粟縣官，而內受錢於都內。

translations.literal: All Bashu taxes were insufficient to replace these costs, so wealthy households were recruited to farm southern tribes, submitting grain to county offices while receiving cash inside the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Fiscal strain forced hybrid colonization-finance schemes linking frontier grain and metropolitan payments.

sentences.zh: 東置滄海郡，人徒之費疑於南夷。

translations.literal: In the east, Canghai commandery was established, with labor costs similar to southern tribes.
translations.idiomatic: Eastern territorial expansion produced comparable labor-fiscal burdens.

sentences.zh: 又興十餘萬築衛朔方，轉漕甚遠，自山東咸被其勞，費數十百鉅萬，府庫並虛。

translations.literal: Again over one hundred thousand were raised to build and guard Shuofang; long-distance transport was extreme, all east-of-mountains bore its burden, costing tens and hundreds of millions, and treasuries emptied.
translations.idiomatic: Mass frontier construction and transport drained central finances and broad provincial labor.

sentences.zh: 乃募民能入奴婢得以終身復，為郎增秩，及入羊為郎，始於此。

translations.literal: Then people able to submit slaves could receive lifelong corvée exemption; becoming lang and rank increases, and submitting sheep for lang began here.
translations.idiomatic: To raise resources, the state converted human and livestock contributions into office entry and labor exemptions.

sentences.zh: 此後四年，衛青比歲十餘萬眾擊胡，斬捕首虜之士受賜黃金二十餘萬斤，而漢軍士馬死者十餘萬，兵甲轉漕之費不與焉。

translations.literal: In the following four years, each year Wei Qing led over one hundred thousand to strike Hu; captured-head soldiers received over two hundred thousand jin of gold rewards, while over one hundred thousand Han soldiers and horses died, excluding armor and transport costs.
translations.idiomatic: Campaign rewards were enormous, casualties massive, and full logistics costs even higher than recorded.

sentences.zh: 於是大司農陳臧錢經用，賦稅既竭，不足以奉戰士。

translations.literal: Then Grand Agriculture Chen calculated money usage; taxes and levies were already exhausted and insufficient to support war troops.
translations.idiomatic: Fiscal accounting showed ordinary revenues could no longer sustain the war machine.

sentences.zh: 有司請令民得買爵及贖禁錮免臧罪；

translations.literal: Officials requested allowing people to buy rank and redeem prohibitions, immunities, and confiscation crimes;
translations.idiomatic: The court turned to monetized rank sales and legal redemption mechanisms.

sentences.zh: 請置賞官，名曰武功爵。

translations.literal: requested establishing reward offices named Military-Merit Rank.
translations.idiomatic: A formal 'Military Merit Rank' purchasing channel was created.

sentences.zh: 級十七萬，凡直三十餘萬金。

translations.literal: Its 17 levels were priced in total at over three hundred thousand jin of gold.
translations.idiomatic: The ladder had seventeen purchasable grades with very high aggregate valuation.

sentences.zh: 諸買武功爵官首者試補吏，先除；

translations.literal: Those first purchasing military-merit office heads were tried and then filled as officials, with priority appointment;
translations.idiomatic: Buyers could receive probationary appointment with accelerated placement.

sentences.zh: 千夫如五大夫；

translations.literal: a thousand-man level equivalent to Five Dafu;
translations.idiomatic: Some grades mapped directly to established noble ranks;

sentences.zh: 其有罪又減二等；

translations.literal: those with crimes could further reduce by two degrees;
translations.idiomatic: legal penalties could also be mitigated through purchase.

sentences.zh: 爵得至樂卿，以顯軍功。

translations.literal: ranks could reach Yueqing to display military merit.
translations.idiomatic: Upper purchase grades reached high honorific offices.

sentences.zh: 軍功多用超等，大者封侯卿大夫，小者郎。

translations.literal: Military merit often used above-grade promotion: great ones enfeoffed marquis or high ministers, lesser ones made lang.
translations.idiomatic: War service and purchased status together accelerated irregular promotion pathways.

sentences.zh: 吏道雜而多端，則官職秏廢。

translations.literal: When official pathways are mixed and many-ended, offices are consumed and discarded.
translations.idiomatic: Institutional coherence decays when appointment channels multiply without hierarchy.

sentences.zh: 自孫弘以春秋之義繩臣下取漢相，張湯以峻文決理為廷尉，於是見知之法生，而廢格沮誹窮治之獄用矣。

translations.literal: From Sun Hong using Spring and Autumn standards to bind ministers and become Han chancellor, and Zhang Tang using severe textual law as chief justice, then laws of 'seen and known' arose, and prosecutions for obstruction, disparagement, and exhaustive investigation were used.
translations.idiomatic: Moralist and legalist techniques fused into a harsher prosecutorial regime, including broad guilt-by-knowledge doctrines.

sentences.zh: 其明年，淮南、衡山、江都王謀反跡見，而公卿尋端治之，竟其黨與，坐而死者數萬人，吏益慘急而法令察。

translations.literal: The next year, traces of rebellion by Huainan, Hengshan, and Jiangdu kings appeared; ministers pursued leads and prosecuted through all their associates, with tens of thousands convicted to death; officials became more cruel and urgent and laws more scrutinizing.
translations.idiomatic: Royal conspiracy trials expanded into sweeping network purges, intensifying administrative severity.

sentences.zh: 當是時，招尊方正賢良文學之士，或至公卿大夫。

translations.literal: At that time, upright and learned worthies were also recruited, some reaching ministers and grandees.
translations.idiomatic: At the same time, the court still promoted some orthodox scholars into high office.

sentences.zh: 公孫弘以宰相，布被，食不重味，為下先，然而無益於俗，稍務於功利矣。

translations.literal: With Gongsun Hong as chancellor, though he wore plain cloth and ate simply as example, he did not improve social custom; policy increasingly turned to utility and profit.
translations.idiomatic: Even under Gongsun Hong's austere personal style, court policy kept drifting toward utilitarian gain.

sentences.zh: 其明年，票騎仍再出擊胡，大克獲。

translations.literal: The next year, the General of Agile Cavalry again campaigned against the Hu and won great captures.
translations.idiomatic: The following year brought another major anti-Hu victory by the elite cavalry command.

sentences.zh: 渾邪王率數萬眾來降，於是漢發車三萬兩迎之。

translations.literal: King Hunxie led tens of thousands to surrender, and Han dispatched thirty thousand wagons to receive them.
translations.idiomatic: When Hunxie surrendered with a huge following, Han mounted a massive reception logistics operation.

sentences.zh: 既至，受賞，賜及有功之士。

translations.literal: After arrival, rewards were granted, including to meritorious soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: The surrender was followed by broad distributions of reward.

sentences.zh: 是歲費凡百餘鉅萬。

translations.literal: That year total costs exceeded one hundred million.
translations.idiomatic: The fiscal cost for that single year surpassed one hundred million.

sentences.zh: 先是十餘歲，河決，灌梁、楚地，固已數困，而緣河之郡隄塞河，輒壞決，費不可勝計。

translations.literal: More than ten years earlier, the Yellow River had breached and flooded Liang and Chu, already causing repeated distress; and dikes in river commanderies repeatedly failed, with costs beyond calculation.
translations.idiomatic: Long before this, recurrent Yellow River failures had already imposed enormous reconstruction costs.

sentences.zh: 其後番係欲省底柱之漕，穿汾、河渠以為溉田；

translations.literal: Later Fan Xi sought to reduce transport at Dizhu and cut Fen-Huang canals for irrigation;
translations.idiomatic: Officials kept proposing hydraulic works to cut transport burdens and expand irrigation.

sentences.zh: 鄭當時為渭漕回遠，鑿漕直渠自長安至華陰；

translations.literal: Zheng Dangshi, because Wei transport detoured far, cut a straight canal from Chang'an to Huayin;
translations.idiomatic: Another project shortened grain routes by direct canal engineering.

sentences.zh: 而朔方亦穿溉渠。

translations.literal: and Shuofang also dug irrigation canals.
translations.idiomatic: Frontier regions likewise undertook large irrigation works.

sentences.zh: 作者各數萬人，歷二三期而功未就，費亦各以鉅萬十數。

translations.literal: Each project used tens of thousands of laborers over two or three terms, still unfinished, each costing over tens of millions.
translations.idiomatic: These projects were labor-intensive, prolonged, and extraordinarily expensive.

sentences.zh: 天子為伐胡故，盛養馬，馬之往來食長安者數萬匹，卒掌者關中不足，乃調旁近郡。

translations.literal: Because of campaigns against the Hu, the emperor heavily raised horses; horses coming and feeding in Chang'an numbered tens of thousands. Guanzhong handlers were insufficient, so nearby commanderies were drafted.
translations.idiomatic: Military horse policy imposed heavy provisioning and manpower burdens across surrounding regions.

sentences.zh: 而胡降者數萬人皆得厚賞，衣食仰給縣官，縣官不給，天子乃損膳，解乘輿駟，出御府禁臧以澹之。

translations.literal: And tens of thousands of surrendered Hu all received generous rewards, their food and clothing supplied by county offices; when counties could not supply, the emperor reduced his own meals, untethered imperial team horses, and released forbidden-treasury stores to support them.
translations.idiomatic: Refugee-surrender maintenance strained local budgets so deeply that even court reserves were tapped.

sentences.zh: 其明年，山東被水災，民多飢乏，於是天子遣使虛郡國倉廩以振貧。

translations.literal: The next year, east of the passes suffered flood disaster and many were hungry; the emperor sent envoys to empty commandery and state granaries for relief.
translations.idiomatic: Widespread flood famine forced large-scale emergency granary releases.

sentences.zh: 猶不足，又募豪富人相假貸。

translations.literal: Still not enough, he further recruited wealthy households for mutual lending.
translations.idiomatic: Even state relief was insufficient, requiring private credit mobilization.

sentences.zh: 尚不能相救，乃徙貧民於關以西，及充朔方以南新秦中，七十餘萬口，衣食皆仰給於縣官。

translations.literal: Still unable to save all, he relocated over seven hundred thousand poor people to west of the passes and to newly settled Qinzhong south of Shuofang, all fed and clothed by county offices.
translations.idiomatic: Mass relocation and state-supported resettlement became a core famine response.

sentences.zh: 數歲，貸與產業，使者分部護，冠蓋相望，費以億計，縣官大空。

translations.literal: For several years they were lent support and productive assets; envoys were divided by sectors to supervise, canopies and carriages lined the roads, costs counted in hundreds of millions, and county finances were emptied.
translations.idiomatic: Sustained resettlement administration consumed vast funds and hollowed local treasuries.

sentences.zh: 而富商賈或墆財役貧，轉轂百數，廢居居邑，封君皆氐首仰給焉。

translations.literal: Meanwhile rich merchants hoarded wealth and exploited the poor, caravans in hundreds of axles, idling in cities; even enfeoffed lords bowed and depended on them.
translations.idiomatic: Merchant-financier power grew so great that even titled elites relied on it.

sentences.zh: 冶鑄煮鹽，財或累萬金，而不佐公家之急，黎民重困。

translations.literal: In smelting and salt-boiling, fortunes reached tens of thousands of gold, yet they did not aid state emergency, and the common people grew more burdened.
translations.idiomatic: Private industrial fortunes expanded while public crisis financing remained unsupported.

sentences.zh: 於是天子與公卿議，更造錢幣以澹用，而摧浮淫并兼之徒。

translations.literal: Therefore the emperor discussed with ministers to remake coinage to meet expenditures and strike speculative, extravagant, annexing groups.
translations.idiomatic: Coin reform was framed as both fiscal necessity and anti-speculation policy.

sentences.zh: 是時禁苑有白鹿而少府多銀錫。

translations.literal: At that time the imperial park had white deer and the lesser treasury had much silver and tin.
translations.idiomatic: Available symbolic and metal resources enabled experimentation with new token currencies.

sentences.zh: 自孝文更造四銖錢，至是歲四十餘年，從建元以來，用少，縣官往往即多銅山而鑄錢，民亦盜鑄，不可勝數。

translations.literal: From Emperor Wen's four-zhu recast to this year, over forty years, and since Jianyuan expenditures were low; county offices often minted where copper mountains were many, and people also counterfeited without count.
translations.idiomatic: Decades of decentralized minting plus private counterfeit had destabilized monetary quality.

sentences.zh: 錢益多而輕，物益少而貴。

translations.literal: Coins grew more numerous and lighter, while goods became fewer and more expensive.
translations.idiomatic: The result was classic debasement inflation: more coin, weaker value, higher prices.

sentences.zh: 有司言曰：「

translations.literal: Officials said:
translations.idiomatic: Officials then proposed corrective policy.

sentences.zh: 古者皮幣，諸侯以聘享。

translations.literal: 'In antiquity leather currency was used by feudal lords for visits and offerings.
translations.idiomatic: They invoked archaic ceremonial currency precedents.

sentences.zh: 金有三等，黃金為上，白金為中，赤金為下。

translations.literal: Gold had three grades: yellow highest, white middle, red lowest.
translations.idiomatic: They proposed a tiered precious-metal hierarchy.

sentences.zh: 今半兩錢法重四銖，而姦或盜摩錢質而取鋊，錢益輕薄而物貴，則遠方用幣煩費不省。」

translations.literal: Now half-liang legally should weigh four zhu, but criminals scrape coin metal and take meltings; coins get thinner and prices rise, so distant regions incur cumbersome currency costs.'
translations.idiomatic: Officials argued clipping and shaving had degraded coin integrity and raised transaction costs.

sentences.zh: 乃以白鹿皮方尺，緣以繢，為皮幣，直四十萬。

translations.literal: So they made white deer skin one chi square, edged with embroidery, as leather currency valued at 400,000.
translations.idiomatic: A high-value ceremonial leather note was introduced.

sentences.zh: 王侯宗室朝覲聘享，必以皮幣薦璧，然後得行。

translations.literal: Princes and imperial clan in audience and ritual had to present jade through leather currency before proceeding.
translations.idiomatic: Use of this token was made mandatory in high-level court ritual exchange.

sentences.zh: 又造銀錫白金。

translations.literal: They also made silver-tin white-metal currency.
translations.idiomatic: Additional white-metal denominations were issued.

sentences.zh: 以為天用莫如龍，地用莫如馬，人用莫如龜，故白金三品：其一曰重八兩，圜之，其文龍，名「白撰」，直三千；

translations.literal: Saying Heaven's highest use is dragon, Earth's is horse, humanity's is turtle, they made three white-metal classes: first eight liang, round, dragon motif, called 'white selected,' worth 3,000;
translations.idiomatic: The new white-metal series used cosmological iconography and fixed face values.

sentences.zh: 二曰以重差小，方之，其文馬，直五百；

translations.literal: second somewhat lighter, square, horse motif, worth 500;
translations.idiomatic: A mid-tier square horse issue was set at 500.

sentences.zh: 三曰復小，橢之，其文龜，直三百。

translations.literal: third smaller, oval, turtle motif, worth 300.
translations.idiomatic: A smaller oval turtle issue was set at 300.

sentences.zh: 令縣官銷半兩錢，更鑄三誅錢，重如其文。

translations.literal: County offices were ordered to melt half-liang coins and recast three-zhu coins with stated weight.
translations.idiomatic: Legacy coin was withdrawn for a new three-zhu standard.

sentences.zh: 盜鑄諸金錢罪皆死，而吏民之犯者不可勝數。

translations.literal: Counterfeiting all metal coin types carried death, yet offenders among officials and people were countless.
translations.idiomatic: Despite capital punishment, counterfeiting remained pervasive.

sentences.zh: 於是以東郭咸陽、孔僅為大農丞，領鹽鐵事，而桑弘羊貴幸。

translations.literal: Thus Dongguo Xianyang and Kong Jin became deputy grand-agriculture officials over salt and iron, and Sang Hongyang rose in favor.
translations.idiomatic: Control over salt-iron-fiscal operations was centralized under technocratic appointees.

sentences.zh: 咸陽，齊之大煮鹽，孔僅，南陽大冶，皆致產累千金，故鄭當時進言之。

translations.literal: Xianyang was Qi's great salt boiler, Kong Jin Nanyang's great smelter, both having accumulated thousands in gold, hence Zheng Dangshi recommended them.
translations.idiomatic: Both appointees were private-sector magnates recruited for state fiscal operations.

sentences.zh: 弘羊，洛陽賈人之子，以心計，年十三侍中。

translations.literal: Hongyang, son of a Luoyang merchant, skilled in calculation, served at court at thirteen.
translations.idiomatic: Sang Hongyang was a prodigious merchant-trained calculator elevated early.

sentences.zh: 故三人言利事析秋豪矣。

translations.literal: Thus these three analyzed profit matters down to fine hairs.
translations.idiomatic: Together they brought highly granular fiscal analytics to policy.

sentences.zh: 法既益嚴，吏多廢免。

translations.literal: As law grew stricter, many officials were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Legal tightening produced bureaucratic churn and purge.

sentences.zh: 兵革數動，民多買復及五大夫、千夫，徵發之士益鮮。

translations.literal: With repeated warfare, many people bought exemptions and ranks, so conscription-eligible men grew fewer.
translations.idiomatic: Rank purchase and exemption markets eroded military manpower pools.

sentences.zh: 於是除千夫、五大夫為吏，不欲者出馬；

translations.literal: Therefore thousand-man and five-dafu ranks were removed from office pathways; those unwilling had to provide horses.
translations.idiomatic: Policy tried to rebalance by limiting office conversion and demanding in-kind military support.

sentences.zh: 故吏皆適令伐棘上林，作昆明池。

translations.literal: So officials were all directed to clear brambles in Shanglin and build Kunming Lake.
translations.idiomatic: Labor was redirected to major state works, including Kunming Lake.

sentences.zh: 其明年，大將軍、票騎大出擊胡，賞賜五十萬金，軍馬死者十餘萬匹，轉漕車甲之費不與焉。

translations.literal: Next year grand general and agile-cavalry commander campaigned hugely against Hu, awarding 500,000 gold; military horses dead exceeded 100,000, excluding transport and equipment costs.
translations.idiomatic: Another campaign brought massive expenditure and horse attrition beyond already huge reward payouts.

sentences.zh: 是時財匱，戰士頗不得祿矣。

translations.literal: At this time finances were depleted, and soldiers often did not receive full pay.
translations.idiomatic: By then fiscal strain was severe enough to disrupt military compensation.

sentences.zh: 有司言三銖錢輕，輕錢易作姦詐，乃更請郡國鑄五銖錢，周郭其質，令不可得摩取鉛。

translations.literal: Officials said three-zhu coin was too light and enabled fraud; they requested commanderies recast five-zhu coin with rim and border so lead could not be scraped.
translations.idiomatic: A heavier anti-clipping five-zhu coin was proposed and standardized with design safeguards.

sentences.zh: 大農上鹽鐵丞孔僅、咸陽言：「山海，天地之臧，宜屬少府，陛下弗私，以屬大農佐賦。

translations.literal: Grand Agriculture submitted salt-iron deputies Kong Jin and Xianyang saying: 'Mountains and seas are Heaven-Earth's stores and should belong to the lesser treasury; Your Majesty does not privatize but assigns to Grand Agriculture to assist taxation.
translations.idiomatic: They argued natural-resource rents should be state-administered for fiscal stabilization.

sentences.zh: 願募民自給費，因官器作煮鹽，官與牢盆。

translations.literal: We request recruiting people to bear their own costs, using official tools for salt-boiling, with officials providing iron pans.
translations.idiomatic: Proposal: licensed private operation under state tooling and controlled inputs.

sentences.zh: 浮食奇民欲擅斡山海之貨，以致富羨，役利細民。

translations.literal: Floating profiteers seek to monopolize mountain-sea goods, achieving excess wealth and exploiting petty people.
translations.idiomatic: Unregulated intermediaries, they said, were extracting monopoly rents from basic resources.

sentences.zh: 其沮事之議，不可勝聽。

translations.literal: Obstructive arguments against this cannot be fully listened to.
translations.idiomatic: They dismissed opposition as endless and obstructive.

sentences.zh: 敢私鑄鐵器煮鹽者，釱左趾，沒入其器物。

translations.literal: Those daring to privately cast iron tools or boil salt: shackle left foot and confiscate equipment.
translations.idiomatic: Severe penalties and confiscation were proposed for illicit iron/salt production.

sentences.zh: 郡不出鐵者，置小鐵官，使屬在所縣。」

translations.literal: In commanderies without iron output, establish small iron offices under local counties.'
translations.idiomatic: Administrative infrastructure would be extended even to low-output regions.

sentences.zh: 使僅、咸陽乘傳舉行天下鹽鐵，作官府，除故鹽鐵家富者為吏。

translations.literal: Kong Jin and Xianyang were sent by relay to implement salt and iron empire-wide, making offices and replacing former wealthy salt-iron families as officials.
translations.idiomatic: State takeover was executed nationwide through new offices staffed partly by former industry elites.

sentences.zh: 吏益多賈人矣。

translations.literal: Officials consequently included even more merchants.
translations.idiomatic: The bureaucracy became increasingly merchantized.

sentences.zh: 商賈以幣之變，多積貨逐利。

translations.literal: Merchants, due to currency changes, accumulated goods and chased profit.
translations.idiomatic: Monetary volatility encouraged speculative inventory behavior.

sentences.zh: 於是公卿言：「郡國頗被災害，貧民無產業者，募徙廣饒之地。

translations.literal: Then ministers said: 'Commanderies and states have suffered disasters; poor people without livelihood should be recruited to migrate to broad and fertile lands.
translations.idiomatic: Policy debates shifted to disaster relief through organized migration.

sentences.zh: 陛下損膳省用，出禁錢以振元元，寬貸，而民不齊出南畝，商賈滋眾。

translations.literal: Your Majesty reduced meals and spending, released forbidden funds to aid the people, and broadened loans, yet people still did not uniformly go out to fields, while merchants multiplied.
translations.idiomatic: Despite austerity and relief, agrarian participation lagged and commerce expanded.

sentences.zh: 貧者畜積無有，皆仰縣官。

translations.literal: The poor have no reserves and all depend on county offices.
translations.idiomatic: Poor households remained structurally dependent on state relief.

sentences.zh: 異時算軺車賈人之嬢錢皆有差，請算如故。

translations.literal: Formerly carriage-tax and merchants' declaration-money all had grades; request to calculate as before.
translations.idiomatic: They proposed restoring graded assessment systems on transport and merchant capital.

sentences.zh: 諸賈人末作貰貸賣買，居邑貯積諸物，及商以取利者，雖無市籍，各以其物自占，率嬢錢二千而算一。

translations.literal: All merchants engaged in lending, buying and selling, urban storage, and trade for profit-even without market registration-must self-declare goods and pay one levy per 2,000 declaration-cash.
translations.idiomatic: A broad self-assessment tax base was imposed across registered and unregistered commercial activity.

sentences.zh: 諸作有租及鑄，率嬢錢四千算一。

translations.literal: All productive operations with rent and casting pay one levy per 4,000 declaration-cash.
translations.idiomatic: Industrial/rental activities were taxed at a different declaration ratio.

sentences.zh: 非吏比者、三老、北邊騎士，軺車一算；

translations.literal: Except ranked officials, village elders, and northern frontier cavalry, one carriage equals one levy;
translations.idiomatic: Most groups owed one levy per carriage, with limited exemptions.

sentences.zh: 商賈人軺車二算；

translations.literal: merchant carriages two levies;
translations.idiomatic: Merchant transport faced double rates.

sentences.zh: 船五丈以上一算。

translations.literal: boats over five zhang one levy.
translations.idiomatic: Larger vessels were similarly taxed.

sentences.zh: 匿不自占，占不悉，戍邊一歲，沒入嬢錢。

translations.literal: Concealing without declaration or incomplete declaration: one year frontier garrison and confiscation of declaration-cash.
translations.idiomatic: Tax evasion triggered military penal service plus confiscation.

sentences.zh: 有能告者，以其半畀之。

translations.literal: Those able to report violations receive half.
translations.idiomatic: Whistleblowers were rewarded with half the confiscated amount.

sentences.zh: 賈人有市籍，及家屬，皆無得名田，以便農。

translations.literal: Merchants with market registration and their households may not hold titled farmland, to support farming convenience.
translations.idiomatic: Registered merchants and family members were barred from titled farmland to protect agrarian structure.

sentences.zh: 敢犯令，沒入田貨。」

translations.literal: Those daring violate order: confiscate land and goods.'
translations.idiomatic: Violations carried full asset forfeiture.

sentences.zh: 是時，豪富皆爭匿財，唯卜式數求入財以助縣官。

translations.literal: At this time wealthy households all competed to hide wealth; only Bu Shi repeatedly requested to contribute assets to aid county offices.
translations.idiomatic: Most elites concealed assets, while Bu Shi stood out for voluntary public contribution.

sentences.zh: 天子乃超拜式為中郎，賜爵左庶長，田十頃，布告天下，以風百姓。

translations.literal: The emperor then exceptionally appointed Shi as palace gentleman, granted rank Left Common Chief and ten qing of land, and announced to all under heaven to influence the people.
translations.idiomatic: Bu Shi was publicly rewarded and showcased as a moral exemplar.

sentences.zh: 初，式不願為官，上強拜之，稍遷至齊相。

translations.literal: Initially Shi did not wish office, but the emperor forced appointment; he was gradually promoted to Qi chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: Though reluctant, Bu Shi was repeatedly advanced into high office.

sentences.zh: 語自在其傳。

translations.literal: The details are in his biography.
translations.idiomatic: Further details are recorded in his separate biography.

sentences.zh: 孔僅使天下鑄作器，三年中至大司農，列於九卿。

translations.literal: Kong Jin made empire-wide casting and manufacturing, and within three years reached Grand Agriculture and ranked among Nine Ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Kong Jin rose rapidly through the fiscal-administrative hierarchy.

sentences.zh: 而桑弘羊為大司農中丞，管諸會計事，稍稍置均輸以通貨物。

translations.literal: Sang Hongyang became deputy grand-agriculture controller, managing accounting affairs and gradually establishing equal-transport to circulate goods.
translations.idiomatic: Sang institutionalized the 'equal transport' system to rebalance commodity flows and revenue.

sentences.zh: 始令吏得入穀補官，郎至六百石。

translations.literal: It began allowing officials to submit grain to fill offices, up to 600-shi rank for gentlemen.
translations.idiomatic: Office access via grain contribution expanded into mid-level ranks.

sentences.zh: 自造白金五銖錢後五歲，而赦吏民之坐盜鑄金錢死者數十萬人。

translations.literal: Five years after creating white-metal and five-zhu coin, they pardoned officials and people sentenced to death for coin-theft and illicit minting, numbering tens of thousands.
translations.idiomatic: After years of harsh enforcement, a massive amnesty was issued for coin-related capital cases.

sentences.zh: 其不發覺相殺者，不可勝計。

translations.literal: Those not discovered and mutually killing cannot be counted.
translations.idiomatic: Undetected violence around minting crimes likely far exceeded official figures.

sentences.zh: 赦自出者百餘萬人。

translations.literal: Those who self-surrendered in amnesty exceeded one million.
translations.idiomatic: Self-report amnesty participation was enormous.

sentences.zh: 然不能半自出，天下大氐無慮皆鑄金錢矣。

translations.literal: Yet it was still less than half self-surrendering; under heaven basically all without concern were casting coin.
translations.idiomatic: Even that scale implied illicit minting had become near-universal.

sentences.zh: 犯法者眾，吏不能盡誅，於是遣博士褚大、徐偃等分行郡國，舉并兼之徒守相為利者。

translations.literal: With offenders many and officials unable to execute all, doctors Chu Da and Xu Yan were sent through commanderies to identify annexers and governors protecting profit.
translations.idiomatic: Unable to enforce universally, the state shifted to targeted investigations of major profiteers and complicit officials.

sentences.zh: 而御史大夫張湯方貴用事，減宣、杜周等為中丞，義縱、尹齊、王溫舒等用急刻為九卿，直指夏蘭之屬始出。

translations.literal: At this time Censor-in-chief Zhang Tang was in power; reducing Xuan and Du Zhou as deputies; Yi Zong, Yin Qi, Wang Wenshu used severe urgency as nine ministers; direct investigators like Xia Lan first emerged.
translations.idiomatic: A hardline prosecutorial cadre rose under Zhang Tang, expanding direct-investigation mechanisms.

sentences.zh: 而大農顏異誅矣。

translations.literal: And Grand Agriculture Yan Yi was executed.
translations.idiomatic: Senior official Yan Yi was executed amid this legal crackdown.

sentences.zh: 初，異為濟南亭長，以廉直稍遷至九卿。

translations.literal: Originally Yi was Jinan pavilion chief, gradually promoted by integrity to Nine Ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Yi had risen through reputation for uprightness before his fall.

sentences.zh: 上與湯既造白鹿皮幣，問異。

translations.literal: After the emperor and Tang made white deer skin currency, they asked Yi.
translations.idiomatic: When consulted on the new leather-currency policy, Yan Yi gave candid criticism.

sentences.zh: 異曰：「今王侯朝賀以倉璧，直數千，而其皮薦反四十萬，本末不相稱。」

translations.literal: Yi said: 'Now princes come court with jade walls worth several thousand, but the skin wrapper is instead four hundred thousand-value and substance are mismatched.'
translations.idiomatic: He argued the token wrapper's face value was absurdly detached from the underlying object.

sentences.zh: 天子不說。

translations.literal: The emperor was displeased.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor took offense.

sentences.zh: 湯又與異有隙，及人有告異以它議，事下湯治。

translations.literal: Tang also had grudge with Yi; when someone accused Yi on another matter, case was sent to Tang for handling.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang exploited the opportunity through procedural prosecution.

sentences.zh: 異與客語，客語初令下有不便者，異不應，微反脣。

translations.literal: Yi spoke with a guest; guest said the newly issued order had inconveniences; Yi did not answer, only slightly curled his lips.
translations.idiomatic: A minor gesture of disapproval became prosecutorial evidence.

sentences.zh: 湯奏當異九卿見令不便，不入言而腹非，論死。

translations.literal: Tang memorialized that Yi as Nine Minister seeing order inconvenient yet not speaking and inwardly criticizing should be sentenced to death.
translations.idiomatic: He was condemned for 'internal dissent' despite no formal remonstrance.

sentences.zh: 自是後有腹非之法比，而公卿大夫多諂諛取容。

translations.literal: From this arose legal precedent of 'belly-slashing criticism,' and ministers and grandees mostly flattered for favor.
translations.idiomatic: This case entrenched a climate where private disagreement became criminal and open flattery became survival strategy.

sentences.zh: 天下既下嬢錢令而尊卜式，百姓終莫分財佐縣官，於是告嬢錢縱矣。

translations.literal: After declaration-money order and honoring Bu Shi, people still never shared wealth to aid county offices, so informing on declaration-money violations became widespread.
translations.idiomatic: Because voluntary contribution failed, enforcement shifted to mass denunciation incentives.

sentences.zh: 郡國鑄錢，民多姦鑄，錢多輕，而公卿請令京師鑄官赤仄，一當五，賦官用非赤仄不得行。

translations.literal: Commanderies cast coin and people widely counterfeited; coin was many and light, and ministers requested capital cast official red-rim coin, one worth five, and official payments not in red-rim coin could not circulate.
translations.idiomatic: A new high-denomination red-rim state coin was imposed as mandatory for official transactions.

sentences.zh: 白金稍賤，民弗寶用，縣官以令禁之，無益，歲餘終廢不行。

translations.literal: White-metal gradually depreciated; people did not value or use it; county offices tried to ban this by order, useless, and after over a year it was finally abolished.
translations.idiomatic: The white-metal issue failed in practice and was eventually abandoned.

sentences.zh: 是歲，湯死而民不思。

translations.literal: In that year Zhang Tang died and the people did not miss him.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang's death drew little public mourning.

sentences.zh: 其後二歲，赤仄錢賤，民巧法用之，不便，又廢。

translations.literal: Two years later red-rim coin also depreciated; people exploited legal tricks with it, inconvenient, and it too was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: The red-rim reform likewise failed and was withdrawn.

sentences.zh: 於是悉禁郡國毋鑄錢，專令上林三官鑄。

translations.literal: Then all commanderies and states were prohibited from casting coin, and only Shanglin three offices were allowed to cast.
translations.idiomatic: Minting was recentralized under the Shanglin Three Offices monopoly.

sentences.zh: 錢既多，而令天下非三官錢不得行，諸郡國前所鑄錢皆廢銷之，輸入其銅三官。

translations.literal: Though coin was plentiful, the order made all under heaven unable to circulate except Three-Office coin; prior commandery coin all abolished and melted, copper delivered into Three Offices.
translations.idiomatic: Compulsory remonetization withdrew local coin and centralized copper feedstock.

sentences.zh: 而民之鑄錢益少，計其費不能相當，唯真工大姦乃盜為之。

translations.literal: As a result people's coin casting decreased, since costs no longer matched returns; only true skilled great criminals still made illicitly.
translations.idiomatic: The monopoly cut ordinary illicit minting, leaving only high-skill organized counterfeiters.

sentences.zh: 楊可告嬢遍天下，中家以上大氐皆遇告。

translations.literal: Yang Ke's declaration-money prosecutions spread across the realm; basically all middle households and above were implicated.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Ke's campaign implicated most middle-and-above households.

sentences.zh: 杜周治之，獄少反者。

translations.literal: Du Zhou handled these cases, with few acquittals.
translations.idiomatic: Under Du Zhou, conviction rates were extremely high.

sentences.zh: 乃分遣御史廷尉正監分曹往，往即治郡國嬢錢，得民財物以億計，奴婢以千萬數，田大縣數百頃，小縣百餘頃，宅亦如之。

translations.literal: Then censors, chief-justice deputies, and supervisors were dispatched in divisions; wherever they went they prosecuted declaration-money cases, obtaining private wealth counted in hundreds of millions, slaves in tens of millions, fields from hundreds of qing in large counties to over one hundred in small, and houses similarly.
translations.idiomatic: Nationwide fiscal prosecutions seized enormous assets-land, houses, people, and cash-on a near-unprecedented scale.

sentences.zh: 於是商賈中家以上大氐破，民媮甘食好衣，不事畜臧之業，而縣官以鹽鐵嬢錢之故，用少饒矣。

translations.literal: As a result, most merchant households above middling means were ruined. People grew fond of rich food and fine clothes and no longer pursued storing wealth through farming; meanwhile, because of revenues from salt, iron, and coinage, government expenditures were somewhat easier to meet.
translations.idiomatic: As a result, most merchant households above middling means were ruined. People grew fond of rich food and fine clothes and no longer pursued storing wealth through farming; meanwhile, because of revenues from salt, iron, and coinage, government expenditures were somewhat easier to meet.

sentences.zh: 益廣開，置左右輔。

translations.literal: The parks were expanded further, and the Left and Right Auxiliary districts were established.
translations.idiomatic: The parks were expanded further, and the Left and Right Auxiliary districts were established.

sentences.zh: 初，大農幹鹽鐵官布多，置水衡，欲以主鹽鐵；

translations.literal: At first, because the Grand Minister of Agriculture managed many salt-and-iron offices, the Office of Waterworks was set up to take charge of them;
translations.idiomatic: At first, because the Grand Minister of Agriculture managed many salt-and-iron offices, the Office of Waterworks was set up to take charge of them;

sentences.zh: 及楊可告嬢，上林財物眾，乃令水衡主上林。

translations.literal: then, after Yang Ke's informer campaign and as assets in Shanglin multiplied, Waterworks was ordered to administer Shanglin.
translations.idiomatic: then, after Yang Ke's informer campaign and as assets in Shanglin multiplied, Waterworks was ordered to administer Shanglin.

sentences.zh: 上林既充滿，益廣。

translations.literal: Once Shanglin was filled, it was expanded still further.
translations.idiomatic: Once Shanglin was filled, it was expanded still further.

sentences.zh: 是時粵欲與漢用船戰逐，乃大修昆明池，列館環之。

translations.literal: At this time Yue was preparing to fight Han by naval warfare, so Kunming Lake was greatly enlarged, with pavilions built in a ring around it.
translations.idiomatic: At this time Yue was preparing to fight Han by naval warfare, so Kunming Lake was greatly enlarged, with pavilions built in a ring around it.

sentences.zh: 治樓船，高十餘丈，旗織加其上，甚壯。

translations.literal: Tower ships were built, over ten zhang high, with woven banners raised above them, a very imposing sight.
translations.idiomatic: Tower ships were built, over ten zhang high, with woven banners raised above them, a very imposing sight.

sentences.zh: 於是天子感之，乃作柏梁臺，高數十丈。

translations.literal: Moved by this, the Son of Heaven then built the Boliang Terrace, dozens of zhang high.
translations.idiomatic: Moved by this, the Son of Heaven then built the Boliang Terrace, dozens of zhang high.

sentences.zh: 宮室之修，繇此日麗。

translations.literal: From this point on, palace construction grew more and more magnificent by the day.
translations.idiomatic: From this point on, palace construction grew more and more magnificent by the day.

sentences.zh: 乃分嬢錢諸官，而水衡、少府、太僕、大農各置農官，往往即郡縣比沒入田田之。

translations.literal: Counterfeiters' confiscated money was divided among offices, and Waterworks, the Lesser Treasury, the Grand Coachman, and the Grand Minister of Agriculture each set up agricultural offices, often taking over confiscated fields in commanderies and counties to farm them.
translations.idiomatic: Counterfeiters' confiscated money was divided among offices, and Waterworks, the Lesser Treasury, the Grand Coachman, and the Grand Minister of Agriculture each set up agricultural offices, often taking over confiscated fields in commanderies and counties to farm them.

sentences.zh: 其沒入奴婢，分諸苑養狗馬禽獸，及與諸官。

translations.literal: Confiscated male and female slaves were distributed among the imperial parks to raise dogs, horses, birds, and beasts, and also assigned to various offices.
translations.idiomatic: Confiscated male and female slaves were distributed among the imperial parks to raise dogs, horses, birds, and beasts, and also assigned to various offices.

sentences.zh: 官益雜置多，徒奴婢眾，而下河漕度四百萬石，及官自糴乃足。

translations.literal: Government offices became ever more numerous and mixed, with many convict laborers and slaves; four million shi had to be shipped by canal downriver, and only with additional government grain purchases was supply sufficient.
translations.idiomatic: Government offices became ever more numerous and mixed, with many convict laborers and slaves; four million shi had to be shipped by canal downriver, and only with additional government grain purchases was supply sufficient.

sentences.zh: 所忠言：「世家子弟富人或鬥雞走狗馬，弋獵博戲，亂齊民。」

translations.literal: Suo Zhong said: 'Sons of great families and rich men sometimes indulge in cockfighting, racing dogs and horses, hunting, and gambling, throwing commoners into disorder.'
translations.idiomatic: Suo Zhong said: 'Sons of great families and rich men sometimes indulge in cockfighting, racing dogs and horses, hunting, and gambling, throwing commoners into disorder.'

sentences.zh: 乃徵諸犯令，相引數千人，名曰「株送徒」。

translations.literal: So offenders were summoned and implicated one another, amounting to several thousand people, called 'chain-sent convicts by clan implication.'
translations.idiomatic: So offenders were summoned and implicated one another, amounting to several thousand people, called 'chain-sent convicts by clan implication.'

sentences.zh: 入財者得補郎，郎選衰矣。

translations.literal: Those who contributed property could be appointed palace gentlemen, and the quality of selected gentlemen declined.
translations.idiomatic: Those who contributed property could be appointed palace gentlemen, and the quality of selected gentlemen declined.

sentences.zh: 是時山東被河災，及歲不登數年，人或相食，方二三千里。

translations.literal: At that time, east of the mountains suffered Yellow River floods, and after several years of bad harvests people even ate one another across an area of two to three thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, east of the mountains suffered Yellow River floods, and after several years of bad harvests people even ate one another across an area of two to three thousand li.

sentences.zh: 天子憐之，令飢民得流就食江淮間，欲留，留處。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven pitied them and ordered famine victims to be allowed to migrate and seek food in the Jiang and Huai region; those who wished to stay could settle there.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven pitied them and ordered famine victims to be allowed to migrate and seek food in the Jiang and Huai region; those who wished to stay could settle there.

sentences.zh: 使者冠蓋相屬於道護之，下巴蜀粟以振焉。

translations.literal: Envoys with official carriages and escorts lined the roads to protect them, and grain from Ba and Shu was sent down to provide relief.
translations.idiomatic: Envoys with official carriages and escorts lined the roads to protect them, and grain from Ba and Shu was sent down to provide relief.

sentences.zh: 明年，天子始出巡郡國。

translations.literal: The next year, the Son of Heaven began touring the commanderies and kingdoms.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, the Son of Heaven began touring the commanderies and kingdoms.

sentences.zh: 東度河，河東守不意行至，不辯，自殺。

translations.literal: He crossed east over the Yellow River; the governor of Hedong had not expected his arrival, failed to prepare, and killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: He crossed east over the Yellow River; the governor of Hedong had not expected his arrival, failed to prepare, and killed himself.

sentences.zh: 行西踰隴，卒，從官不得食，隴西守自殺。

translations.literal: Traveling west across Long, the soldiers and attendants ran short of food, and the governor of Longxi killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: Traveling west across Long, the soldiers and attendants ran short of food, and the governor of Longxi killed himself.

sentences.zh: 於是上北出蕭關，從數萬騎行獵新秦中，以勒邊兵而歸。

translations.literal: Then the emperor went north out through Xiao Pass, led tens of thousands of cavalry on a hunt in New Qin Central, reviewed frontier troops, and returned.
translations.idiomatic: Then the emperor went north out through Xiao Pass, led tens of thousands of cavalry on a hunt in New Qin Central, reviewed frontier troops, and returned.

sentences.zh: 新秦中或千里無亭徼，於是誅北地太守以下，而令民得畜邊縣，官假馬母，三歲而歸，及息什一，以除告嬢，用充入新秦中。

translations.literal: In parts of New Qin Central there were stretches of a thousand li without posts or watch stations. So officials from the governor of Beidi downward were executed, and people were ordered to raise livestock in frontier counties. The government lent brood mares, to be returned after three years with one-tenth interest, and penalties from informer prosecutions were remitted, to fill up New Qin Central.
translations.idiomatic: In parts of New Qin Central there were stretches of a thousand li without posts or watch stations. So officials from the governor of Beidi downward were executed, and people were ordered to raise livestock in frontier counties. The government lent brood mares, to be returned after three years with one-tenth interest, and penalties from informer prosecutions were remitted, to fill up New Qin Central.

sentences.zh: 既得寶鼎，立后土、泰一祠，公卿白議封禪事，而郡國皆豫治道，修繕故宮，及當馳道縣，縣治宮儲，設共具，而望幸。

translations.literal: After obtaining the precious tripod, the emperor established altars to Houtu and Taiyi. Ministers and grandees formally discussed the Feng and Shan rites, while commanderies and kingdoms all prepared roads, repaired old palaces, and counties on imperial routes readied palace stores and provisions in hopes of an imperial visit.
translations.idiomatic: After obtaining the precious tripod, the emperor established altars to Houtu and Taiyi. Ministers and grandees formally discussed the Feng and Shan rites, while commanderies and kingdoms all prepared roads, repaired old palaces, and counties on imperial routes readied palace stores and provisions in hopes of an imperial visit.

sentences.zh: 明年，南粵反，西羌侵邊。

translations.literal: The next year, Nanyue rebelled and the Western Qiang raided the frontier.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, Nanyue rebelled and the Western Qiang raided the frontier.

sentences.zh: 天子為山東不澹，赦天下囚，因南方樓船士二十餘萬人擊粵，發三河以西騎擊羌，又數萬人度河築令居。

translations.literal: Because the region east of the mountains was still not settled, the Son of Heaven pardoned prisoners throughout the realm, then sent over 200,000 southern naval troops to attack Yue, dispatched cavalry from west of the Three Rivers to strike the Qiang, and sent tens of thousands more across the river to build Lingju.
translations.idiomatic: Because the region east of the mountains was still not settled, the Son of Heaven pardoned prisoners throughout the realm, then sent over 200,000 southern naval troops to attack Yue, dispatched cavalry from west of the Three Rivers to strike the Qiang, and sent tens of thousands more across the river to build Lingju.

sentences.zh: 初置張掖、酒泉郡，而上郡、朔方、西河、河西開田官，斥塞卒六十萬人戍田之。

translations.literal: Zhangye and Jiuquan commanderies were first established, and in Shangjun, Shuofang, Xihe, and Hexi agricultural garrison offices were opened, with 600,000 frontier soldiers stationed to farm.
translations.idiomatic: Zhangye and Jiuquan commanderies were first established, and in Shangjun, Shuofang, Xihe, and Hexi agricultural garrison offices were opened, with 600,000 frontier soldiers stationed to farm.

sentences.zh: 中國繕道餽糧，遠者三千，近者千餘里，皆仰給大農。

translations.literal: Within the central states, roads were repaired and grain was shipped to the fronts: up to 3,000 li for distant sectors and over 1,000 li for nearer ones, all supplied by the Grand Minister of Agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: Within the central states, roads were repaired and grain was shipped to the fronts: up to 3,000 li for distant sectors and over 1,000 li for nearer ones, all supplied by the Grand Minister of Agriculture.

sentences.zh: 邊兵不足，乃發武庫工官兵器以澹之。

translations.literal: Frontier troops were insufficient, so weapons from imperial arsenals and workshops were issued to make up the shortfall.
translations.idiomatic: Frontier troops were insufficient, so weapons from imperial arsenals and workshops were issued to make up the shortfall.

sentences.zh: 車騎馬乏，縣官錢少，買馬難得，乃著令，令封君以下至三百石吏以上差出

translations.literal: Chariots, cavalry, and horses were lacking, and the government had little cash, so buying horses was difficult. An edict therefore required ranks from enfeoffed lords down to officials of 300-shi salary and above to contribute by grade
translations.idiomatic: Chariots, cavalry, and horses were lacking, and the government had little cash, so buying horses was difficult. An edict therefore required ranks from enfeoffed lords down to officials of 300-shi salary and above to contribute by grade

sentences.zh: 牡馬天下亭，亭有畜字馬，歲課息。

translations.literal: stallions to post stations throughout the empire; each station kept breeding-marked horses and reported yearly increase.
translations.idiomatic: stallions to post stations throughout the empire; each station kept breeding-marked horses and reported yearly increase.

sentences.zh: 齊相卜式上書，願父子死南粵。

translations.literal: Bu Shi, the Chancellor of Qi, submitted a memorial asking that he and his sons be allowed to die fighting in Nanyue.
translations.idiomatic: Bu Shi, the Chancellor of Qi, submitted a memorial asking that he and his sons be allowed to die fighting in Nanyue.

sentences.zh: 天子下詔褒揚，賜爵關內侯，黃金四十斤，田十頃。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven issued an edict praising him and granted him the rank of Marquis Within the Passes, forty jin of gold, and ten qing of land.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven issued an edict praising him and granted him the rank of Marquis Within the Passes, forty jin of gold, and ten qing of land.

sentences.zh: 布告天下，天下莫應。

translations.literal: This was proclaimed throughout the empire, but no one responded.
translations.idiomatic: This was proclaimed throughout the empire, but no one responded.

sentences.zh: 列侯以百數，皆莫求從軍。

translations.literal: There were hundreds of marquises, yet none sought to join the army.
translations.idiomatic: There were hundreds of marquises, yet none sought to join the army.

sentences.zh: 至飲酎，少府省金，而列侯坐酎金失侯者百餘人。

translations.literal: At the libation ceremony, when the Lesser Treasury audited gold contributions, more than a hundred marquises lost their titles for shorting their libation gold.
translations.idiomatic: At the libation ceremony, when the Lesser Treasury audited gold contributions, more than a hundred marquises lost their titles for shorting their libation gold.

sentences.zh: 乃拜卜式為御史大夫。

translations.literal: Bu Shi was then appointed Censor-in-Chief.
translations.idiomatic: Bu Shi was then appointed Censor-in-Chief.

sentences.zh: 式既在位，見郡國多不便縣官作鹽鐵，器苦惡，賈貴，或彊令民買之。

translations.literal: Once in office, Bu Shi saw that many commanderies and kingdoms found government-run salt and iron burdensome: goods were poor, prices high, and people were sometimes forced to buy them.
translations.idiomatic: Once in office, Bu Shi saw that many commanderies and kingdoms found government-run salt and iron burdensome: goods were poor, prices high, and people were sometimes forced to buy them.

sentences.zh: 而船有算，商者少，物貴，乃因孔僅言船算事。

translations.literal: A levy was also imposed on boats, reducing merchants and driving up prices; this followed Kong Jin's proposal on boat taxation.
translations.idiomatic: A levy was also imposed on boats, reducing merchants and driving up prices; this followed Kong Jin's proposal on boat taxation.

sentences.zh: 上不說。

translations.literal: The emperor was displeased.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was displeased.

sentences.zh: 漢連出兵三歲，誅羌，滅兩粵，番禺以西至蜀南者置初郡十七，且以其故俗治，無賦稅。

translations.literal: Han had campaigned continuously for three years, punishing the Qiang and destroying the two Yue states. From Panyu westward to south of Shu, seventeen new commanderies were established and governed according to local customs, with no taxes.
translations.idiomatic: Han had campaigned continuously for three years, punishing the Qiang and destroying the two Yue states. From Panyu westward to south of Shu, seventeen new commanderies were established and governed according to local customs, with no taxes.

sentences.zh: 南陽、漢中以往，各以地比給初郡吏卒奉食幣物，傳車馬被具。

translations.literal: Regions from Nanyang and Hanzhong onward each supplied support to officials and troops in the new commanderies, along with relay carts, horses, clothing, and equipment.
translations.idiomatic: Regions from Nanyang and Hanzhong onward each supplied support to officials and troops in the new commanderies, along with relay carts, horses, clothing, and equipment.

sentences.zh: 而初郡又時時小反，殺吏，漢發南方吏卒往誅之，間歲萬餘人，費皆仰大農。

translations.literal: Yet the new commanderies repeatedly had minor rebellions and killings of officials. Han sent southern officials and troops to suppress them; in alternate years over ten thousand men were mobilized, and all expenses were borne by the Grand Minister of Agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: Yet the new commanderies repeatedly had minor rebellions and killings of officials. Han sent southern officials and troops to suppress them; in alternate years over ten thousand men were mobilized, and all expenses were borne by the Grand Minister of Agriculture.

sentences.zh: 大農以均輸調鹽鐵助賦，故能澹之。

translations.literal: The Grand Minister, through balanced transport and salt-and-iron adjustments, was able to sustain these costs.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Minister, through balanced transport and salt-and-iron adjustments, was able to sustain these costs.

sentences.zh: 然兵所過縣，縣以為訾給毋乏而已，不敢言輕賦法矣。

translations.literal: Still, counties through which the armies passed could only provide enough by assessed quotas to avoid shortages, and no one dared speak of lighter taxes anymore.
translations.idiomatic: Still, counties through which the armies passed could only provide enough by assessed quotas to avoid shortages, and no one dared speak of lighter taxes anymore.

sentences.zh: 其明年，元封元年，卜式貶為太子太傅。

translations.literal: The following year, the first year of Yuanfeng, Bu Shi was demoted to Tutor to the Crown Prince.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, the first year of Yuanfeng, Bu Shi was demoted to Tutor to the Crown Prince.

sentences.zh: 而桑弘羊為治粟都尉，領大農，盡代僅斡天下鹽鐵。

translations.literal: Sang Hongyang became Superintendent for Grain and concurrently headed the Grand Minister of Agriculture, fully replacing Kong Jin in managing the empire's salt and iron.
translations.idiomatic: Sang Hongyang became Superintendent for Grain and concurrently headed the Grand Minister of Agriculture, fully replacing Kong Jin in managing the empire's salt and iron.

sentences.zh: 弘羊以諸官各自市相爭，物以故騰躍，而天下賦輸或不償其僦費，乃請置大農部丞數十人，分部主郡國，各往往置均輸鹽鐵官，令遠方各以其物如異時商賈所轉

translations.literal: Hongyang argued that because offices purchased goods on their own and competed with one another, prices soared, and tax deliveries from the realm sometimes failed even to cover transport costs. He therefore requested dozens of assistant directors under the Grand Minister, divided by region to oversee commanderies and kingdoms, and in many places to establish balanced-transport and salt-and-iron offices, so distant areas could use local products as merchants formerly did when moving goods
translations.idiomatic: Hongyang argued that because offices purchased goods on their own and competed with one another, prices soared, and tax deliveries from the realm sometimes failed even to cover transport costs. He therefore requested dozens of assistant directors under the Grand Minister, divided by region to oversee commanderies and kingdoms, and in many places to establish balanced-transport and salt-and-iron offices, so distant areas could use local products as merchants formerly did when moving goods

sentences.zh: 貶者為賦，而相灌輸。

translations.literal: as tax payments according to comparative value, with mutual transfer and distribution.
translations.idiomatic: as tax payments according to comparative value, with mutual transfer and distribution.

sentences.zh: 置平準於京師，都受天下委輸。

translations.literal: A price-stabilization office was established in the capital to receive consignments from all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: A price-stabilization office was established in the capital to receive consignments from all under Heaven.

sentences.zh: 召工官治車諸器，皆仰給大農。

translations.literal: Artisan offices were summoned to make carts and various implements, all funded by the Grand Minister of Agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: Artisan offices were summoned to make carts and various implements, all funded by the Grand Minister of Agriculture.

sentences.zh: 大農諸官盡籠天下之貨物，貴則賣之，賤則買之。

translations.literal: The Grand Minister's offices gathered control of goods across the realm: when prices were high they sold, and when prices were low they bought.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Minister's offices gathered control of goods across the realm: when prices were high they sold, and when prices were low they bought.

sentences.zh: 如此，富商大賈亡所牟大利，則反本，而萬物不得騰躍。

translations.literal: In this way, rich merchants and great traders had no room for huge profits, returned to their roots, and prices of all goods could not surge.
translations.idiomatic: In this way, rich merchants and great traders had no room for huge profits, returned to their roots, and prices of all goods could not surge.

sentences.zh: 故抑天下之物，名曰「平準」。

translations.literal: Thus goods across the empire were kept in check; this was called 'price stabilization.'
translations.idiomatic: Thus goods across the empire were kept in check; this was called 'price stabilization.'

sentences.zh: 天子以為然而許之。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven approved and authorized it.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven approved and authorized it.

sentences.zh: 於是天子北至朔方，東封泰山，巡海上，旁北邊以歸。

translations.literal: Then the emperor went north to Shuofang, east to perform the Feng rite at Mount Tai, toured along the seacoast, and returned by way of the northern frontier.
translations.idiomatic: Then the emperor went north to Shuofang, east to perform the Feng rite at Mount Tai, toured along the seacoast, and returned by way of the northern frontier.

sentences.zh: 所過賞賜，用帛百餘萬匹，錢金以鉅萬計，皆取足大農。

translations.literal: Along the route he granted rewards, spending over a million bolts of silk, with money and gold counted in tens of millions, all drawn from the Grand Minister of Agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: Along the route he granted rewards, spending over a million bolts of silk, with money and gold counted in tens of millions, all drawn from the Grand Minister of Agriculture.

sentences.zh: 弘羊又請令民得入粟補吏，及罪以贖。

translations.literal: Hongyang also requested allowing people to deliver grain in exchange for official posts, and to redeem punishments with payment.
translations.idiomatic: Hongyang also requested allowing people to deliver grain in exchange for official posts, and to redeem punishments with payment.

sentences.zh: 令民入粟甘泉各有差，以復終身，不復告嬢。

translations.literal: People were ordered to deliver grain to Ganquan by set grades, in return for lifelong exemption and no further informer prosecutions.
translations.idiomatic: People were ordered to deliver grain to Ganquan by set grades, in return for lifelong exemption and no further informer prosecutions.

sentences.zh: 它郡各輸急處，而諸農各致粟，山東漕益歲六百萬石。

translations.literal: Other commanderies each sent supplies to urgent fronts, and the agricultural offices all delivered grain; annual grain transport from east of the mountains rose by six million shi.
translations.idiomatic: Other commanderies each sent supplies to urgent fronts, and the agricultural offices all delivered grain; annual grain transport from east of the mountains rose by six million shi.

sentences.zh: 一歲之中，太倉、甘泉倉滿。

translations.literal: Within a single year, the Taicang and Ganquan granaries were full.
translations.idiomatic: Within a single year, the Taicang and Ganquan granaries were full.

sentences.zh: 邊餘穀，諸均輸帛五百萬匹。

translations.literal: The frontier had surplus grain, and the balanced-transport offices held five million bolts of silk.
translations.idiomatic: The frontier had surplus grain, and the balanced-transport offices held five million bolts of silk.

sentences.zh: 民不益賦而天下用饒。

translations.literal: The people bore no increase in taxes, yet state finances were ample.
translations.idiomatic: The people bore no increase in taxes, yet state finances were ample.

sentences.zh: 於是弘羊賜爵左庶長，黃金者再百焉。

translations.literal: Accordingly, Hongyang was granted the rank of Left Commoner Chief and another two hundred jin of gold.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, Hongyang was granted the rank of Left Commoner Chief and another two hundred jin of gold.

sentences.zh: 是歲小旱，上令百官求雨。

translations.literal: That year there was a slight drought, and the emperor ordered all officials to pray for rain.
translations.idiomatic: That year there was a slight drought, and the emperor ordered all officials to pray for rain.

sentences.zh: 卜式言曰：「縣官當食租衣稅而已，今弘羊令吏坐市列，販物求利。

translations.literal: Bu Shi said: 'Government should subsist on land-tax grain and tax cloth alone. Now Hongyang has officials sitting in market rows, trading goods for profit.
translations.idiomatic: Bu Shi said: 'Government should subsist on land-tax grain and tax cloth alone. Now Hongyang has officials sitting in market rows, trading goods for profit.

sentences.zh: 亨弘羊，天乃雨。」

translations.literal: Boil Hongyang alive, and then Heaven will send rain.'
translations.idiomatic: Boil Hongyang alive, and then Heaven will send rain.'

sentences.zh: 久之，武帝疾病，拜弘羊為御史大夫。

translations.literal: After some time, when Emperor Wu fell ill, Hongyang was appointed Censor-in-Chief.
