translations.idiomatic: After some time, when Emperor Wu fell ill, Hongyang was appointed Censor-in-Chief.

sentences.zh: 昭帝即位六年，詔郡國舉賢良文學之士，問以民所疾苦，教化之要。

translations.literal: In the sixth year after Emperor Zhao took the throne, an edict ordered commanderies and kingdoms to recommend worthy and learned scholars and to ask them about the people's hardships and the essentials of moral instruction.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year after Emperor Zhao took the throne, an edict ordered commanderies and kingdoms to recommend worthy and learned scholars and to ask them about the people's hardships and the essentials of moral instruction.

sentences.zh: 皆對願罷鹽鐵酒鸾均輸官，毋與天下爭利，視以儉節，然後教化可興。

translations.literal: They all replied that the salt, iron, liquor-monopoly, and balanced-transport offices should be abolished; the state should not compete with the people for profit, and by modeling thrift and restraint moral transformation could then flourish.
translations.idiomatic: They all replied that the salt, iron, liquor-monopoly, and balanced-transport offices should be abolished; the state should not compete with the people for profit, and by modeling thrift and restraint moral transformation could then flourish.

sentences.zh: 弘羊難，以為此國家大業，所以制四夷，安邊足用之本，不可廢也。

translations.literal: Hongyang challenged this, arguing that these were major state enterprises, the basis for controlling the Four Barbarians, securing the frontiers, and ensuring adequate resources, and therefore could not be abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Hongyang challenged this, arguing that these were major state enterprises, the basis for controlling the Four Barbarians, securing the frontiers, and ensuring adequate resources, and therefore could not be abolished.

sentences.zh: 乃與丞相千秋共奏罷酒酤。

translations.literal: He then jointly memorialized with Chancellor Qianqiu to abolish the liquor monopoly.
translations.idiomatic: He then jointly memorialized with Chancellor Qianqiu to abolish the liquor monopoly.

sentences.zh: 弘羊自以為國興大利，伐其功，欲為子弟得官，怨望大將軍霍光，遂與上官桀等謀反，誅滅。

translations.literal: Hongyang claimed he had brought great profit to the state and boasted of his achievements; he sought offices for his sons and younger kin, resented General-in-Chief Huo Guang, and so plotted rebellion with Shangguan Jie and others, for which he and his clan were exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: Hongyang claimed he had brought great profit to the state and boasted of his achievements; he sought offices for his sons and younger kin, resented General-in-Chief Huo Guang, and so plotted rebellion with Shangguan Jie and others, for which he and his clan were exterminated.

sentences.zh: 宣、元、成、哀、平五世，亡所變改。

translations.literal: Across the five reigns of Xuan, Yuan, Cheng, Ai, and Ping, nothing was changed.
translations.idiomatic: Across the five reigns of Xuan, Yuan, Cheng, Ai, and Ping, nothing was changed.

sentences.zh: 元帝時嘗罷鹽鐵官，三年而復之。

translations.literal: During Emperor Yuan's reign, the salt-and-iron offices were once abolished, but they were restored after three years.
translations.idiomatic: During Emperor Yuan's reign, the salt-and-iron offices were once abolished, but they were restored after three years.

sentences.zh: 貢禹言：「鑄錢采銅，一歲十萬人不耕，民坐盜鑄陷刑者多。

translations.literal: Gong Yu said: 'Minting coin and mining copper keeps one hundred thousand men each year from farming, and many people are punished for illicit coining.
translations.idiomatic: Gong Yu said: 'Minting coin and mining copper keeps one hundred thousand men each year from farming, and many people are punished for illicit coining.

sentences.zh: 富人臧錢滿室，猶無厭足。

translations.literal: The rich hoard rooms full of cash and still are never satisfied.
translations.idiomatic: The rich hoard rooms full of cash and still are never satisfied.

sentences.zh: 民心動搖，棄本逐末，耕者不能半，姦邪不可禁，原起於錢。

translations.literal: People's hearts are unsettled: they abandon the root and chase the branch; fewer than half remain farmers; wickedness cannot be curbed. The source is money.
translations.idiomatic: People's hearts are unsettled: they abandon the root and chase the branch; fewer than half remain farmers; wickedness cannot be curbed. The source is money.

sentences.zh: 疾其末者絕其本，宜罷采珠玉金銀鑄錢之官，毋復以為幣，除其販賣租銖之律，租稅祿賜皆以布帛及穀，使百姓壹意農桑。」

translations.literal: If you want to stop the branch, cut off the root. The offices for mining pearls, jade, gold, and silver and minting coin should be abolished; coin should no longer serve as currency; laws taxing tiny monetary units in trade should be removed; and rents, taxes, salaries, and rewards should all be paid in cloth and grain, so the people can focus wholly on farming and sericulture.'
translations.idiomatic: If you want to stop the branch, cut off the root. The offices for mining pearls, jade, gold, and silver and minting coin should be abolished; coin should no longer serve as currency; laws taxing tiny monetary units in trade should be removed; and rents, taxes, salaries, and rewards should all be paid in cloth and grain, so the people can focus wholly on farming and sericulture.'

sentences.zh: 議者以為交易待錢，布帛不可尺寸分裂。

translations.literal: Discussants replied that exchange depends on money, and cloth cannot be divided precisely by inch and foot.
translations.idiomatic: Discussants replied that exchange depends on money, and cloth cannot be divided precisely by inch and foot.

sentences.zh: 禹議亦寢。

translations.literal: So Yu's proposal was shelved.
translations.idiomatic: So Yu's proposal was shelved.

sentences.zh: 自孝武元狩五年三官初鑄五銖錢，至平帝元始中，成錢二百八十億萬餘云。

translations.literal: From the fifth year of Yuanshou under Emperor Wu, when the Three Offices first cast wuzhu coins, to the Zhong era of Yuanshi under Emperor Ping, more than 2.8 trillion coins were minted, it is said.
translations.idiomatic: From the fifth year of Yuanshou under Emperor Wu, when the Three Offices first cast wuzhu coins, to the Zhong era of Yuanshi under Emperor Ping, more than 2.8 trillion coins were minted, it is said.

sentences.zh: 王莽居攝，變漢制，以周錢有子母相權，於是更造大錢，徑寸二分，重十二銖，文曰「大錢五十」。

translations.literal: When Wang Mang served as regent, he altered Han institutions. Since Zhou coinage had mother-and-child denominations balancing one another, he newly cast a large coin, one cun and two fen in diameter and twelve zhu in weight, inscribed 'Large Coin Fifty.'
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Mang served as regent, he altered Han institutions. Since Zhou coinage had mother-and-child denominations balancing one another, he newly cast a large coin, one cun and two fen in diameter and twelve zhu in weight, inscribed 'Large Coin Fifty.'

sentences.zh: 又造契刀、錯刀。

translations.literal: He also cast the Contract Knife and the Inlaid Knife.
translations.idiomatic: He also cast the Contract Knife and the Inlaid Knife.

sentences.zh: 契刀，其環如大錢，身形如刀，長二寸，文曰「契刀五百」。

translations.literal: The Contract Knife had a ring like the large coin and a knife-shaped body, two cun long, inscribed 'Contract Knife Five Hundred.'
translations.idiomatic: The Contract Knife had a ring like the large coin and a knife-shaped body, two cun long, inscribed 'Contract Knife Five Hundred.'

sentences.zh: 錯刀，以黃金錯其文，曰「一刀直五千」。

translations.literal: On the Inlaid Knife, the inscription was inlaid with gold and read, 'One Knife Worth Five Thousand.'
translations.idiomatic: On the Inlaid Knife, the inscription was inlaid with gold and read, 'One Knife Worth Five Thousand.'

sentences.zh: 與五銖錢凡四品，並行。

translations.literal: Together with the wuzhu coin, these made four denominations in circulation.
translations.idiomatic: Together with the wuzhu coin, these made four denominations in circulation.

sentences.zh: 莽即真，以為書「劉」字有金刀，乃罷錯刀、契刀及五銖錢，而更作金、銀、龜、貝、錢、布之品，名曰「寶貨」。

translations.literal: When Mang formally took the throne, he claimed the graph for 'Liu' contained the elements for 'metal' and 'knife,' and so abolished the Inlaid Knife, Contract Knife, and wuzhu coin. He then created categories of gold, silver, turtle shell, cowries, coin, and spade money, calling them 'Precious Currencies.'
translations.idiomatic: When Mang formally took the throne, he claimed the graph for 'Liu' contained the elements for 'metal' and 'knife,' and so abolished the Inlaid Knife, Contract Knife, and wuzhu coin. He then created categories of gold, silver, turtle shell, cowries, coin, and spade money, calling them 'Precious Currencies.'

sentences.zh: 小錢徑六分，重一銖，文曰「小錢直一」。

translations.literal: The small coin had a diameter of six fen and a weight of one zhu, inscribed 'Small Coin Worth One.'
translations.idiomatic: The small coin had a diameter of six fen and a weight of one zhu, inscribed 'Small Coin Worth One.'

sentences.zh: 次七分，三銖，曰「

translations.literal: The next had a diameter of seven fen and a weight of three zhu, called
translations.idiomatic: The next had a diameter of seven fen and a weight of three zhu, called

sentences.zh: 錢一十」。

translations.literal: 'Coin Ten.'
translations.idiomatic: 'Coin Ten.'

sentences.zh: 次八分，五銖，曰「幼錢二十」。

translations.literal: The next had a diameter of eight fen and a weight of five zhu, called 'Young Coin Twenty.'
translations.idiomatic: The next had a diameter of eight fen and a weight of five zhu, called 'Young Coin Twenty.'

sentences.zh: 次九分，七銖，曰「中錢三十」。

translations.literal: The next had a diameter of nine fen and a weight of seven zhu, called 'Middle Coin Thirty.'
translations.idiomatic: The next had a diameter of nine fen and a weight of seven zhu, called 'Middle Coin Thirty.'

sentences.zh: 次一寸，九銖，曰「壯錢四十」。

translations.literal: The next had a diameter of one cun and a weight of nine zhu, called 'Strong Coin Forty.'
translations.idiomatic: The next had a diameter of one cun and a weight of nine zhu, called 'Strong Coin Forty.'

sentences.zh: 因前「大錢五十」，是為錢貨六品，直各如其文。

translations.literal: Together with the earlier 'Large Coin Fifty,' these formed six grades of coin currency, each valued as its inscription stated.
translations.idiomatic: Together with the earlier 'Large Coin Fifty,' these formed six grades of coin currency, each valued as its inscription stated.

sentences.zh: 黃金重一斤，直錢萬。

translations.literal: Gold, at one jin in weight, was worth ten thousand cash.
translations.idiomatic: Gold, at one jin in weight, was worth ten thousand cash.

sentences.zh: 朱提銀重八兩為一流，直一千五百八十。

translations.literal: Zhu-ti silver, at eight liang per unit, was worth 1,580 cash.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu-ti silver, at eight liang per unit, was worth 1,580 cash.

sentences.zh: 它銀一流直千。

translations.literal: Other silver, one unit, was worth 1,000 cash.
translations.idiomatic: Other silver, one unit, was worth 1,000 cash.

sentences.zh: 是為銀貨二品。

translations.literal: These made two grades of silver currency.
translations.idiomatic: These made two grades of silver currency.

sentences.zh: 元龜岠冉長尺二寸，直二千一百六十，為大貝十朋。

translations.literal: A prime turtle shell, one chi two cun long, was worth 2,160 cash, equivalent to ten strings of large cowries.
translations.idiomatic: A prime turtle shell, one chi two cun long, was worth 2,160 cash, equivalent to ten strings of large cowries.

sentences.zh: 公龜九寸，直五百，為壯貝十朋。

translations.literal: A duke-grade turtle shell, nine cun long, was worth five hundred, equivalent to ten strings of strong cowries.
translations.idiomatic: A duke-grade turtle shell, nine cun long, was worth five hundred, equivalent to ten strings of strong cowries.

sentences.zh: 侯龜七寸以上，直三百，為幺貝十朋。

translations.literal: A marquis-grade turtle shell, seven cun or more, was worth three hundred, equivalent to ten strings of lesser cowries.
translations.idiomatic: A marquis-grade turtle shell, seven cun or more, was worth three hundred, equivalent to ten strings of lesser cowries.

sentences.zh: 子龜五寸以上，直百，為小貝十朋。

translations.literal: A son-grade turtle shell, five cun or more, was worth one hundred, equivalent to ten strings of small cowries.
translations.idiomatic: A son-grade turtle shell, five cun or more, was worth one hundred, equivalent to ten strings of small cowries.

sentences.zh: 是為龜寶四品。

translations.literal: These made four grades of turtle currency.
translations.idiomatic: These made four grades of turtle currency.

sentences.zh: 大貝四寸八分以上，二枚為一朋，直二百一十六。

translations.literal: Large cowries, at least four cun and eight fen, two shells to a string, were worth 216 cash.
translations.idiomatic: Large cowries, at least four cun and eight fen, two shells to a string, were worth 216 cash.

sentences.zh: 壯貝三寸六分以上，二枚為一朋，直五十。

translations.literal: Strong cowries, at least three cun and six fen, two shells to a string, were worth fifty.
translations.idiomatic: Strong cowries, at least three cun and six fen, two shells to a string, were worth fifty.

sentences.zh: 幺貝二寸四分以上，二枚為一朋，直三十。

translations.literal: Lesser cowries, at least two cun and four fen, two shells to a string, were worth thirty.
translations.idiomatic: Lesser cowries, at least two cun and four fen, two shells to a string, were worth thirty.

sentences.zh: 小貝寸二分以上，二枚為一朋，直十。

translations.literal: Small cowries, at least one cun and two fen, two shells to a string, were worth ten.
translations.idiomatic: Small cowries, at least one cun and two fen, two shells to a string, were worth ten.

sentences.zh: 不盈寸二分，漏度不得為朋，率枚直錢三。

translations.literal: Those under one cun two fen and below full measure could not be strung; each shell was reckoned at three cash.
translations.idiomatic: Those under one cun two fen and below full measure could not be strung; each shell was reckoned at three cash.

sentences.zh: 是為貝貨五品。

translations.literal: These made five grades of cowry currency.
translations.idiomatic: These made five grades of cowry currency.

sentences.zh: 大布、次布、弟布、壯布、中布、差布、厚布、幼布、幺布、小布。

translations.literal: Great Cloth, Next Cloth, Younger Cloth, Strong Cloth, Middle Cloth, Lesser Cloth, Heavy Cloth, Young Cloth, Tiny Cloth, and Small Cloth.
translations.idiomatic: Great Cloth, Next Cloth, Younger Cloth, Strong Cloth, Middle Cloth, Lesser Cloth, Heavy Cloth, Young Cloth, Tiny Cloth, and Small Cloth.

sentences.zh: 小布長寸五分，重十五銖，文曰「小布一百」。

translations.literal: Small Cloth was one cun five fen long and weighed fifteen zhu, inscribed 'Small Cloth One Hundred.'
translations.idiomatic: Small Cloth was one cun five fen long and weighed fifteen zhu, inscribed 'Small Cloth One Hundred.'

sentences.zh: 自小布以上，各相長一分，相重一銖，文各為其布名，直各加一百。

translations.literal: Above Small Cloth, each grade increased by one fen in length and one zhu in weight; each bore its own cloth-name inscription, and each added one hundred in value.
translations.idiomatic: Above Small Cloth, each grade increased by one fen in length and one zhu in weight; each bore its own cloth-name inscription, and each added one hundred in value.

sentences.zh: 上至大布，長二寸四分，重一兩，而直千錢矣。

translations.literal: At the top, Great Cloth was two cun four fen long, weighed one liang, and was worth one thousand cash.
translations.idiomatic: At the top, Great Cloth was two cun four fen long, weighed one liang, and was worth one thousand cash.

sentences.zh: 是為布貨十品。

translations.literal: These made ten grades of cloth currency.
translations.idiomatic: These made ten grades of cloth currency.

sentences.zh: 凡寶貨五物，六名，二十八品。

translations.literal: In all, the precious currencies used five materials, six names, and twenty-eight denominations.
translations.idiomatic: In all, the precious currencies used five materials, six names, and twenty-eight denominations.

sentences.zh: 鑄作錢布皆用銅，殽以連錫，文質周郭放漢五銖錢云。

translations.literal: All coins and cloth-money were cast in copper alloyed with lead and tin, with inscriptions and rims modeled on Han wuzhu coinage.
translations.idiomatic: All coins and cloth-money were cast in copper alloyed with lead and tin, with inscriptions and rims modeled on Han wuzhu coinage.

sentences.zh: 其金銀與它物雜，色不純好，龜不盈五寸，貝不盈六分，皆不得為寶貨。

translations.literal: If gold and silver were mixed with other substances so their color was impure, or if turtle shells were under five cun or cowries under six fen, they could not be used as precious currency.
translations.idiomatic: If gold and silver were mixed with other substances so their color was impure, or if turtle shells were under five cun or cowries under six fen, they could not be used as precious currency.

sentences.zh: 元龜為蔡，非四民所得居，有者，入大卜受直。

translations.literal: Prime turtle shells belonged to the state treasury and could not be held by the four classes; anyone possessing them had to submit them to the Grand Diviner and receive fixed payment.
translations.idiomatic: Prime turtle shells belonged to the state treasury and could not be held by the four classes; anyone possessing them had to submit them to the Grand Diviner and receive fixed payment.

sentences.zh: 百姓憒亂，其貨不行。

translations.literal: The common people were bewildered, and this currency failed to circulate.
translations.idiomatic: The common people were bewildered, and this currency failed to circulate.

sentences.zh: 民私以五銖錢市買。

translations.literal: People traded privately using wuzhu coins.
translations.idiomatic: People traded privately using wuzhu coins.

sentences.zh: 莽患之，下詔：「敢非井田挾五銖錢者為惑眾，投諸四裔以御魑魅。」

translations.literal: Wang Mang worried over this and issued an edict: 'Whoever opposes the well-field system and keeps wuzhu coins dares to mislead the masses; cast them to the four frontiers to ward off demons and spirits.'
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang worried over this and issued an edict: 'Whoever opposes the well-field system and keeps wuzhu coins dares to mislead the masses; cast them to the four frontiers to ward off demons and spirits.'

sentences.zh: 於是農商失業，食貨俱廢，民涕泣於市道。

translations.literal: As a result, farmers and merchants lost their livelihoods, food and goods systems both collapsed, and people wept in the marketplace roads.
translations.idiomatic: As a result, farmers and merchants lost their livelihoods, food and goods systems both collapsed, and people wept in the marketplace roads.

sentences.zh: 坐賣買田宅奴婢鑄錢抵罪者，自公卿大夫至庶人，不可稱數。

translations.literal: Those convicted for buying and selling fields, houses, slaves, or minting coin ranged from dukes and ministers down to commoners, too many to count.
translations.idiomatic: Those convicted for buying and selling fields, houses, slaves, or minting coin ranged from dukes and ministers down to commoners, too many to count.

sentences.zh: 莽知民愁，乃但行小錢直一，與大錢五十，二品並行，龜貝布屬且寢。

translations.literal: Mang knew the people were distressed, so he allowed only Small Coin One and Large Coin Fifty to circulate as two types, while turtle, cowry, and cloth currencies were suspended.
translations.idiomatic: Mang knew the people were distressed, so he allowed only Small Coin One and Large Coin Fifty to circulate as two types, while turtle, cowry, and cloth currencies were suspended.

sentences.zh: 莽性躁擾，不能無為，每有所興造，必欲依古得經文。

translations.literal: Mang was by nature agitated and restless, unable to let things be; whenever he launched something new, he always wanted classical textual proof.
translations.idiomatic: Mang was by nature agitated and restless, unable to let things be; whenever he launched something new, he always wanted classical textual proof.

sentences.zh: 國師公劉歆言周有泉府之官，收不讎，與欲得，即易所謂「理財正辭，禁民為非」者也。

translations.literal: State Preceptor Liu Xin said that Zhou had an Office of Springs that took in unsold goods and supplied those who wanted them - this was what the Changes called 'ordering finances and rectifying terms, forbidding the people to do wrong.'
translations.idiomatic: State Preceptor Liu Xin said that Zhou had an Office of Springs that took in unsold goods and supplied those who wanted them - this was what the Changes called 'ordering finances and rectifying terms, forbidding the people to do wrong.'

sentences.zh: 莽乃下詔曰：「夫周禮有賒貸，樂語有五均，傳記各有斡焉。

translations.literal: Mang then issued an edict: 'The Rites of Zhou has lending, the music traditions have the Five Equalizations, and transmitted records each have balancing mechanisms.
translations.idiomatic: Mang then issued an edict: 'The Rites of Zhou has lending, the music traditions have the Five Equalizations, and transmitted records each have balancing mechanisms.

sentences.zh: 今開賒貸，張五均，設諸斡者，所以齊眾庶，抑并兼也。」

translations.literal: Now we open lending, expand the Five Equalizations, and establish balancing offices in order to level the common people and restrain annexation by the powerful.'
translations.idiomatic: Now we open lending, expand the Five Equalizations, and establish balancing offices in order to level the common people and restrain annexation by the powerful.'

sentences.zh: 遂於長安及五都立五均官，更名長安東西市令及洛陽、邯鄲、臨甾、宛、成都市長皆為五均司市稱師。

translations.literal: He accordingly established Five Equalization offices in Chang'an and the five capitals, renaming the market chiefs of East and West Chang'an and of Luoyang, Handan, Linzi, Wan, and Chengdu as 'Market Masters' under the Five Equalizations.
translations.idiomatic: He accordingly established Five Equalization offices in Chang'an and the five capitals, renaming the market chiefs of East and West Chang'an and of Luoyang, Handan, Linzi, Wan, and Chengdu as 'Market Masters' under the Five Equalizations.

sentences.zh: 東市稱京，西市稱畿，洛陽稱中，餘四都各用東西南北為稱，皆置交易丞五人，錢府丞一人。

translations.literal: Chang'an East Market was called Jing, West Market Ji, Luoyang Zhong, and the other four capitals each used East, West, South, and North as designations; each had five Exchange Assistants and one Money Treasury Assistant.
translations.idiomatic: Chang'an East Market was called Jing, West Market Ji, Luoyang Zhong, and the other four capitals each used East, West, South, and North as designations; each had five Exchange Assistants and one Money Treasury Assistant.

sentences.zh: 工商能采金銀銅連錫登龜取貝者，皆自占司市錢府，順時氣而取之。

translations.literal: Artisans and merchants able to mine gold, silver, copper, lead, and tin or gather turtle and cowry goods all had to self-register with the market and money offices and obtain them in accord with seasonal timing.
translations.idiomatic: Artisans and merchants able to mine gold, silver, copper, lead, and tin or gather turtle and cowry goods all had to self-register with the market and money offices and obtain them in accord with seasonal timing.

sentences.zh: 又以周官稅民：凡田不耕為不殖，出三夫之稅；

translations.literal: He also taxed people per Zhou offices: any field left uncultivated counted as nonproductive and paid tax equal to three adult males.
translations.idiomatic: He also taxed people per Zhou offices: any field left uncultivated counted as nonproductive and paid tax equal to three adult males.

sentences.zh: 城郭中宅不樹藝者為不毛，出三夫之布；

translations.literal: In walled cities, house lots without trees or crops counted as barren and paid cloth tax equal to three adult males.
translations.idiomatic: In walled cities, house lots without trees or crops counted as barren and paid cloth tax equal to three adult males.

sentences.zh: 民浮游無事，出夫布一匹。

translations.literal: Idle drifters with no occupation paid one bolt of cloth per adult male.
translations.idiomatic: Idle drifters with no occupation paid one bolt of cloth per adult male.

sentences.zh: 其不能出布者，缈作，縣官衣食之。

translations.literal: Those unable to provide cloth performed labor service, with government providing food and clothing.
translations.idiomatic: Those unable to provide cloth performed labor service, with government providing food and clothing.

sentences.zh: 諸取眾物鳥獸魚鱉百蟲於山林水澤及畜牧者，嬪婦桑蠶織紝紡績補縫，工匠醫巫卜祝及它方技商販賈人坐肆列里區謁舍，皆各自占所為於其在所之縣官，除其本，計其利，十一分之，而以其一為貢。

translations.literal: All who took resources from mountains, forests, waters, marshes, or herding; women engaged in mulberry, silkworm, weaving, spinning, mending; craftsmen, physicians, shamans, diviners, ritual specialists, other technicians, merchants, traders, shopkeepers, and inn operators all had to self-register their occupation with local county offices. After deducting principal, one-tenth of profit was taken and one part paid as contribution.
translations.idiomatic: All who took resources from mountains, forests, waters, marshes, or herding; women engaged in mulberry, silkworm, weaving, spinning, mending; craftsmen, physicians, shamans, diviners, ritual specialists, other technicians, merchants, traders, shopkeepers, and inn operators all had to self-register their occupation with local county offices. After deducting principal, one-tenth of profit was taken and one part paid as contribution.

sentences.zh: 敢不自占，自占不以實者，盡沒入所采取，而作縣官一歲。

translations.literal: Those who failed to self-register or registered dishonestly had all collected goods confiscated and had to labor for county offices for one year.
translations.idiomatic: Those who failed to self-register or registered dishonestly had all collected goods confiscated and had to labor for county offices for one year.

sentences.zh: 諸司市常以四時中月實定所掌，為物上中下之賈，各自用為其市平，毋拘它所。

translations.literal: Market offices, in the middle month of each season, regularly verified what they administered and set upper, middle, and lower prices for goods, each using its own local market standard without being bound to other places.
translations.idiomatic: Market offices, in the middle month of each season, regularly verified what they administered and set upper, middle, and lower prices for goods, each using its own local market standard without being bound to other places.

sentences.zh: 眾民賣買五穀布帛絲綿之物，周於民用而不讎者，均官有以考檢厥實，用其本賈取之，毋令折錢。

translations.literal: When people sold and bought grain, cloth, silk floss, and similar necessities and trade did not clear, equalization officials examined the facts and purchased at base price, without forcing discounted cash conversion.
translations.idiomatic: When people sold and bought grain, cloth, silk floss, and similar necessities and trade did not clear, equalization officials examined the facts and purchased at base price, without forcing discounted cash conversion.

sentences.zh: 萬物卬貴，過平一錢，則以平賈賣與民。

translations.literal: When prices rose above the standard by one cash, the office sold to the people at the standard price.
translations.idiomatic: When prices rose above the standard by one cash, the office sold to the people at the standard price.

sentences.zh: 其賈氐賤減平者，聽民自相與市，以防貴庾者。

translations.literal: When prices fell below the standard, people were allowed to trade freely among themselves, to guard against hoarders waiting for high prices.
translations.idiomatic: When prices fell below the standard, people were allowed to trade freely among themselves, to guard against hoarders waiting for high prices.

sentences.zh: 民欲祭祀喪紀而無用者，錢府以所入工商之貢但賒之，祭祀無過旬日，喪紀毋過三月。

translations.literal: If people needed funds for sacrifices or funerals, the money office lent from the industrial and commercial contributions it had received; sacrifices were limited to ten days and funerals to three months.
translations.idiomatic: If people needed funds for sacrifices or funerals, the money office lent from the industrial and commercial contributions it had received; sacrifices were limited to ten days and funerals to three months.

sentences.zh: 民或乏絕，欲貸以治產業者，均授之，除其費，計所得受息，毋過歲什一。

translations.literal: If people were in hardship and sought loans to manage livelihoods, equalization offices issued them; after costs were deducted, charged interest could not exceed one-tenth per year.
translations.idiomatic: If people were in hardship and sought loans to manage livelihoods, equalization offices issued them; after costs were deducted, charged interest could not exceed one-tenth per year.

sentences.zh: 羲和魯匡言：「名山大澤，鹽鐵錢布帛，五均賒貸，斡在縣官，唯酒酤獨未斡。

translations.literal: Xihe official Lu Kuang said: 'Famous mountains and great marshes, salt, iron, coin, cloth and silk, the Five Equalizations, and lending are all under state control; only liquor sales alone are not controlled.
translations.idiomatic: Xihe official Lu Kuang said: 'Famous mountains and great marshes, salt, iron, coin, cloth and silk, the Five Equalizations, and lending are all under state control; only liquor sales alone are not controlled.

sentences.zh: 酒者，天之美祿，帝王所以頤養天下，享祀祈福，扶衰養疾。

translations.literal: Wine is Heaven's fine bounty, what emperors and kings use to nourish all under Heaven, make offerings, pray for blessings, support the weak, and treat illness.
translations.idiomatic: Wine is Heaven's fine bounty, what emperors and kings use to nourish all under Heaven, make offerings, pray for blessings, support the weak, and treat illness.

sentences.zh: 百禮之會，非酒不行。

translations.literal: At every gathering of rites, nothing proceeds without wine.
translations.idiomatic: At every gathering of rites, nothing proceeds without wine.

sentences.zh: 故《詩》曰『無酒酤我』，而論語曰『酤酒不食』，二者非相反也。

translations.literal: Thus the Odes says, "Do not retail me wine," while the Analects says, "Bought wine he did not drink" - these are not contradictory.
translations.idiomatic: Thus the Odes says, "Do not retail me wine," while the Analects says, "Bought wine he did not drink" - these are not contradictory.

sentences.zh: 夫詩據承平之世，酒酤在官，和旨便人，可以相御也。

translations.literal: The Ode speaks from an age of order, when wine retail was in government hands and quality was harmonious and suitable, so it could be consumed with confidence.
translations.idiomatic: The Ode speaks from an age of order, when wine retail was in government hands and quality was harmonious and suitable, so it could be consumed with confidence.

sentences.zh: 論語孔子當周衰亂，酒酤在民，薄惡不誠，是以疑而弗食。

translations.literal: In the Analects, Confucius lived when Zhou was declining and chaotic, with wine retail in private hands; quality was thin, poor, and insincere, so he suspected it and did not drink.
translations.idiomatic: In the Analects, Confucius lived when Zhou was declining and chaotic, with wine retail in private hands; quality was thin, poor, and insincere, so he suspected it and did not drink.

sentences.zh: 今絕天下之酒，則無以行禮相養；

translations.literal: If wine is completely prohibited across the realm now, there will be no means to conduct rites and mutual care;
translations.idiomatic: If wine is completely prohibited across the realm now, there will be no means to conduct rites and mutual care;

sentences.zh: 放而亡限，則費財傷民。

translations.literal: if it is released without limit, wealth will be wasted and the people harmed.
translations.idiomatic: if it is released without limit, wealth will be wasted and the people harmed.

sentences.zh: 請法古，令官作酒，以二千五百石為一均，率開一盧以賣，讎五十釀為準。

translations.literal: Please follow antiquity and have officials brew wine, taking 2,500 shi as one unit and opening one still to sell, using fifty brewings as the standard basis.
translations.idiomatic: Please follow antiquity and have officials brew wine, taking 2,500 shi as one unit and opening one still to sell, using fifty brewings as the standard basis.

sentences.zh: 一釀用麤米二斛，麴一斛，得成酒六斛六斗。

translations.literal: One brewing uses two hu of coarse rice and one hu of yeast, producing six hu six dou of finished wine.
translations.idiomatic: One brewing uses two hu of coarse rice and one hu of yeast, producing six hu six dou of finished wine.

sentences.zh: 各以其市月朔米麴三斛，并計其賈而參分之，以其一為酒一斛之平。

translations.literal: Using the market's first-day monthly price for three hu of rice and yeast combined, divide by three and take one part as the standard price for one hu of wine.
translations.idiomatic: Using the market's first-day monthly price for three hu of rice and yeast combined, divide by three and take one part as the standard price for one hu of wine.

sentences.zh: 除米麴本賈，計其利而什分之，以其七入官，其三及纸酨灰炭給工器薪樵之費。」

translations.literal: After deducting rice and yeast costs, calculate profit and divide into ten parts: seven go to the state; three cover paper, ferment, ash, charcoal, labor, equipment, and fuel costs.'
translations.idiomatic: After deducting rice and yeast costs, calculate profit and divide into ten parts: seven go to the state; three cover paper, ferment, ash, charcoal, labor, equipment, and fuel costs.'

sentences.zh: 羲和置命士督五均六斡，郡有數人，皆用富賈。

translations.literal: Xihe then appointed command aides to supervise the Five Equalizations and Six Controls, with several in each commandery, all chosen from wealthy merchants.
translations.idiomatic: Xihe then appointed command aides to supervise the Five Equalizations and Six Controls, with several in each commandery, all chosen from wealthy merchants.

sentences.zh: 洛陽薛子仲、張長叔、臨菑姓偉等，乘傳求利，交錯天下。

translations.literal: Men such as Xue Zizhong and Zhang Changshu of Luoyang and Xing Wei of Linzi rode relay routes seeking profit and crisscrossed the empire.
translations.idiomatic: Men such as Xue Zizhong and Zhang Changshu of Luoyang and Xing Wei of Linzi rode relay routes seeking profit and crisscrossed the empire.

sentences.zh: 因與郡縣通姦，多張空簿，府臧不實，百姓俞病。

translations.literal: They colluded corruptly with commandery and county offices, widely padded empty ledgers, and made treasuries' holdings false, while the common people suffered ever more.
translations.idiomatic: They colluded corruptly with commandery and county offices, widely padded empty ledgers, and made treasuries' holdings false, while the common people suffered ever more.

sentences.zh: 莽知民苦之，復下詔曰：「夫鹽，食肴之將；

translations.literal: Mang knew the people suffered and again issued an edict: 'Salt is the commander of flavors in food;
translations.idiomatic: Mang knew the people suffered and again issued an edict: 'Salt is the commander of flavors in food;

sentences.zh: 酒，百藥之長，嘉會之好；

translations.literal: wine is the chief of medicines and the joy of noble gatherings;
translations.idiomatic: wine is the chief of medicines and the joy of noble gatherings;

sentences.zh: 鐵，曰農之本；

translations.literal: iron is the foundation of farming;
translations.idiomatic: iron is the foundation of farming;

sentences.zh: 名山大澤，饒衍之臧；

translations.literal: famous mountains and great marshes are storehouses of abundance;
translations.idiomatic: famous mountains and great marshes are storehouses of abundance;

sentences.zh: 五均賒貸，百姓所取平，卬以給澹；

translations.literal: the Five Equalizations and lending are what the people use to obtain fair prices, and when prices rise they supply deficiencies;
translations.idiomatic: the Five Equalizations and lending are what the people use to obtain fair prices, and when prices rise they supply deficiencies;

sentences.zh: 鐵布銅冶，通行有無，備民用也。

translations.literal: iron, cloth, and copper smelting circulate surpluses and shortages and prepare what the people need.
translations.idiomatic: iron, cloth, and copper smelting circulate surpluses and shortages and prepare what the people need.

sentences.zh: 此六者，非編戶齊民所能家作，必卬於市，雖貴數倍，不得不買。

translations.literal: These six are not things registered household commoners can produce at home; they must rely on markets, and even at several times the price they cannot avoid buying them.
translations.idiomatic: These six are not things registered household commoners can produce at home; they must rely on markets, and even at several times the price they cannot avoid buying them.

sentences.zh: 豪民富賈，即要貧弱，先聖知其然也，故斡之。

translations.literal: Powerful clans and rich merchants then coerce the poor and weak; the former sages knew this, and so they controlled them.
translations.idiomatic: Powerful clans and rich merchants then coerce the poor and weak; the former sages knew this, and so they controlled them.

sentences.zh: 每一斡為設科條防禁，犯者罪至死。」

translations.literal: For each controlled sector, rules and prohibitions are set; penalties for violators extend to death.'
translations.idiomatic: For each controlled sector, rules and prohibitions are set; penalties for violators extend to death.'

sentences.zh: 姦吏猾民並侵，眾庶各不安生。

translations.literal: Corrupt officials and crafty commoners encroached together, and all the populace lived without security.
translations.idiomatic: Corrupt officials and crafty commoners encroached together, and all the populace lived without security.

sentences.zh: 後五歲，天鳳元年，復申下金銀龜貝之貨，頗增減其賈直。

translations.literal: Five years later, in the first year of Tianfeng, he again promulgated gold, silver, turtle, and cowry currencies, somewhat increasing or decreasing their set values.
translations.idiomatic: Five years later, in the first year of Tianfeng, he again promulgated gold, silver, turtle, and cowry currencies, somewhat increasing or decreasing their set values.

sentences.zh: 而罷大小錢，改作貨布，長二寸五分，廣一寸，首長八分有奇，廣八分，其圜好徑二分半，足枝長八分，間廣二分，其文右曰「

translations.literal: He abolished large and small coins and instead made the Huo Bu: two cun five fen long and one cun wide, with a head a little over eight fen long and eight fen wide; its round hole was two and a half fen in diameter, and the foot branches were eight fen long with two fen between them. On the right the inscription read
translations.idiomatic: He abolished large and small coins and instead made the Huo Bu: two cun five fen long and one cun wide, with a head a little over eight fen long and eight fen wide; its round hole was two and a half fen in diameter, and the foot branches were eight fen long with two fen between them. On the right the inscription read

sentences.zh: 貨」，左曰「布」，重二十五銖，直貨泉二十五。

translations.literal: 'Huo,' and on the left 'Bu.' It weighed twenty-five zhu and was worth twenty-five Huo Quan.
translations.idiomatic: 'Huo,' and on the left 'Bu.' It weighed twenty-five zhu and was worth twenty-five Huo Quan.

sentences.zh: 貨泉徑一寸，重五銖，文右曰「貨」，左曰「泉」，枚直一，與貨布二品並行。

translations.literal: Huo Quan had a diameter of one cun, weighed five zhu, with 'Huo' on the right and 'Quan' on the left, each coin worth one; it circulated alongside Huo Bu as two denominations.
translations.idiomatic: Huo Quan had a diameter of one cun, weighed five zhu, with 'Huo' on the right and 'Quan' on the left, each coin worth one; it circulated alongside Huo Bu as two denominations.

sentences.zh: 又以大錢行久，罷之，恐民挾不止，乃令民且獨行大錢，與新貨泉俱枚直一，並行盡六年，毋得復挾大錢矣。

translations.literal: Because the large coin had circulated for a long time, when he abolished it he feared people would keep hoarding it. So he ordered people for the time being to circulate only the large coin together with the new Huo Quan, both counted at one each; after six full years of concurrent circulation, they were no longer allowed to hold large coins.
translations.idiomatic: Because the large coin had circulated for a long time, when he abolished it he feared people would keep hoarding it. So he ordered people for the time being to circulate only the large coin together with the new Huo Quan, both counted at one each; after six full years of concurrent circulation, they were no longer allowed to hold large coins.

sentences.zh: 每壹易錢，民用破業，而大陷刑。

translations.literal: Each time the currency was changed, people lost their livelihoods, and many fell deeply into criminal punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Each time the currency was changed, people lost their livelihoods, and many fell deeply into criminal punishment.

sentences.zh: 莽以私鑄錢死，及非沮寶貨投四裔，犯法者多，不可勝行，乃更輕其法：私鑄作泉布者，與妻子沒入為官奴婢；

translations.literal: Mang had previously imposed death for private coin casting and exile to the four frontiers for opposing or obstructing precious currencies. Because offenders became too numerous to punish fully, he reduced the law: private minting of Huo Quan or Huo Bu brought confiscation of offender, wife, and children as government slaves.
translations.idiomatic: Mang had previously imposed death for private coin casting and exile to the four frontiers for opposing or obstructing precious currencies. Because offenders became too numerous to punish fully, he reduced the law: private minting of Huo Quan or Huo Bu brought confiscation of offender, wife, and children as government slaves.

sentences.zh: 吏及比伍，知而不舉告，與同罪；

translations.literal: Officials and neighborhood-group members who knew but failed to report were punished as accomplices.
translations.idiomatic: Officials and neighborhood-group members who knew but failed to report were punished as accomplices.

sentences.zh: 非沮寶貨，民罰作一歲，吏免官。

translations.literal: For opposing or obstructing precious currencies, commoners were penalized with one year of labor, and officials were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: For opposing or obstructing precious currencies, commoners were penalized with one year of labor, and officials were dismissed.

sentences.zh: 犯者俞眾，及五人相坐皆沒入，郡國檻車鐵鎖，傳送長安鍾官，愁苦死者什六七。

translations.literal: Offenders became ever more numerous; once five people were mutually implicated, all were confiscated. Commanderies and kingdoms sent them in prison carts and iron chains to Chang'an's Bell Office, where six or seven in ten died in misery.
translations.idiomatic: Offenders became ever more numerous; once five people were mutually implicated, all were confiscated. Commanderies and kingdoms sent them in prison carts and iron chains to Chang'an's Bell Office, where six or seven in ten died in misery.

sentences.zh: 作貨布六年後，匈奴侵寇甚，莽大募天下囚徒人奴，名曰豬突豨勇，壹切稅吏民，訾三十而取一。

translations.literal: After six years of Huo Bu, Xiongnu raids grew severe. Mang massively conscripted prisoners and privately held slaves from across the realm, calling them 'Boar-Rushing Braves,' and imposed a blanket levy on officials and people alike, taking one in thirty of assessed property.
translations.idiomatic: After six years of Huo Bu, Xiongnu raids grew severe. Mang massively conscripted prisoners and privately held slaves from across the realm, calling them 'Boar-Rushing Braves,' and imposed a blanket levy on officials and people alike, taking one in thirty of assessed property.

sentences.zh: 又令公卿以下至郡縣黃綬吏，皆保養軍馬，吏盡復以與民。

translations.literal: He further ordered all officials from ministers down to county yellow-ribbon clerks to keep and rear military horses, and officials in turn shifted the entire burden to the people.
translations.idiomatic: He further ordered all officials from ministers down to county yellow-ribbon clerks to keep and rear military horses, and officials in turn shifted the entire burden to the people.

sentences.zh: 民搖手觸禁，不得耕桑，繇役煩劇，而枯旱蝗蟲相因。

translations.literal: At the slightest movement people touched prohibitions; they could not farm or raise silkworms, corvee and service were oppressive, and droughts and locusts came one after another.
translations.idiomatic: At the slightest movement people touched prohibitions; they could not farm or raise silkworms, corvee and service were oppressive, and droughts and locusts came one after another.

sentences.zh: 又用制作未定，上自公侯，下至小吏，皆不得奉祿，而私賦斂，貨賂上流，獄訟不決。

translations.literal: With institutions still unsettled, everyone from nobles and marquises down to petty clerks went unpaid, so they imposed private exactions; bribes flowed upward, and lawsuits went unresolved.
translations.idiomatic: With institutions still unsettled, everyone from nobles and marquises down to petty clerks went unpaid, so they imposed private exactions; bribes flowed upward, and lawsuits went unresolved.

sentences.zh: 吏用苛暴立威，旁緣莽禁，侵刻小民。

translations.literal: Officials used harsh violence to establish authority and, taking advantage of Mang's prohibitions, squeezed and carved up the common people.
translations.idiomatic: Officials used harsh violence to establish authority and, taking advantage of Mang's prohibitions, squeezed and carved up the common people.

sentences.zh: 富者不得自保，貧者無以自存，起為盜賊，依阻山澤，吏不能禽而覆蔽之，浸淫日廣，於是青、徐、荊楚之地往往萬數。

translations.literal: The rich could not protect themselves, the poor had no way to survive; they rose as bandits and took refuge in mountains and marshes. Officials could not capture them and even sheltered them, and the spread widened day by day, with forces often numbering in the tens of thousands in Qing, Xu, and Jing-Chu.
translations.idiomatic: The rich could not protect themselves, the poor had no way to survive; they rose as bandits and took refuge in mountains and marshes. Officials could not capture them and even sheltered them, and the spread widened day by day, with forces often numbering in the tens of thousands in Qing, Xu, and Jing-Chu.

sentences.zh: 戰鬥死亡，緣邊四夷所係虜，陷罪，飢疫，人相食，及莽未誅，而天下戶口減半矣。

translations.literal: With battle deaths, border captures by surrounding tribes, criminal convictions, famine, epidemic, and cannibalism, by the time Mang had not yet been executed the empire's households had already been reduced by half.
translations.idiomatic: With battle deaths, border captures by surrounding tribes, criminal convictions, famine, epidemic, and cannibalism, by the time Mang had not yet been executed the empire's households had already been reduced by half.

sentences.zh: 自發豬突豨勇後四年，而漢兵誅莽。

translations.literal: Four years after the Boar-Rushing Braves were first raised, Han forces executed Mang.
translations.idiomatic: Four years after the Boar-Rushing Braves were first raised, Han forces executed Mang.

sentences.zh: 後二年，世祖受命，盪滌煩苛，復五銖錢，與天下更始。

translations.literal: Two years later, the World-Progenitor accepted Heaven's mandate, swept away burdensome harshness, restored wuzhu coins, and gave the realm a fresh beginning.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later, the World-Progenitor accepted Heaven's mandate, swept away burdensome harshness, restored wuzhu coins, and gave the realm a fresh beginning.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：《易》稱「裒多益寡，稱物平施」，《書》云「楙遷有無」，周有泉府之官，而孟子亦非「狗彘食人之食不知斂，野有餓驿而弗知發」。

translations.literal: Appraisal: The Changes says, 'Reduce excess and increase deficiency; weigh things and distribute fairly.' The Documents says, 'Diligently transfer what is lacking and what is abundant.' Zhou had the Office of Springs, and Mencius likewise condemned a ruler who let dogs and swine eat people's food without collecting stores, and let starving corpses lie in the wild without relief.
translations.idiomatic: Appraisal: The Changes says, 'Reduce excess and increase deficiency; weigh things and distribute fairly.' The Documents says, 'Diligently transfer what is lacking and what is abundant.' Zhou had the Office of Springs, and Mencius likewise condemned a ruler who let dogs and swine eat people's food without collecting stores, and let starving corpses lie in the wild without relief.

sentences.zh: 故管氏之輕重，李悝之平糴，弘羊均輸，壽昌常平，亦有從徠。

translations.literal: Therefore Guanzi's light-and-heavy policy, Li Kui's balanced grain purchases, Hongyang's balanced transport, and Shouchang's ever-normal granaries all had precedent.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore Guanzi's light-and-heavy policy, Li Kui's balanced grain purchases, Hongyang's balanced transport, and Shouchang's ever-normal granaries all had precedent.

sentences.zh: 顧古為之有數，吏良而令行，故民賴其利，萬國作乂。

translations.literal: In antiquity such measures had proper limits, with good officials and enforceable orders; therefore people benefited from them and the myriad states were well governed.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity such measures had proper limits, with good officials and enforceable orders; therefore people benefited from them and the myriad states were well governed.

sentences.zh: 及孝武時，國用饒給，而民不益賦，其次也。

translations.literal: By Emperor Wu's time, state expenditures were amply supplied while the people did not bear additional taxes - that was second-best.
translations.idiomatic: By Emperor Wu's time, state expenditures were amply supplied while the people did not bear additional taxes - that was second-best.

sentences.zh: 至于王莽，制度失中，姦軌弄權，官民俱竭，亡次矣。

translations.literal: By Wang Mang's time, institutions lost the middle way, treacherous men manipulated power, officials and people were both exhausted - that was the worst case.
translations.idiomatic: By Wang Mang's time, institutions lost the middle way, treacherous men manipulated power, officials and people were both exhausted - that was the worst case.
